findings.title: Statewide bacteria nowcast beats station memory and public-rule baselines
findings.headline: AP 0.5099 vs station memory 0.2782; ROC-AUC 0.8675; ECE 0.0087
findings.plain: The strongest MBAL result is the California beach-bacteria nowcast in the deployable EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO stratum. On 89,321 held-out 2022+ beach-days with 8,999 exceedances, the calibrated HGBT ranks exceedance risk above station memory, AB411 rain rules, prior-lab history, month climatology, and a Virtual Beach-class MLR baseline.
findings.how: Frozen HGBT with rain, discharge, harmonic tide, station/geography, and lagged history features. The protocol uses train <=2019, calibration 2020-2021, test >=2022, and a fixed 2-day reveal-lag proxy.
findings.note: Retrospective operational nowcast, not a safe-to-swim guarantee. The fixed-lag AP is about 0.02 AP optimistic versus true available_at timing; conservative available_at AP is about 0.4899. San Diego remains excluded from deployment language.

sections.title: Question
sections.body: Can a model rank risky beach-days better than station history and simple public rules?

sections.title: Held-out design
sections.body: Train <=2019; calibration 2020-2021; test >=2022; EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO deployable stratum; fixed 2-day reveal-lag proxy with availability disclosure.

sections.title: Result
sections.body: Calibrated HGBT AP 0.5099 and ROC-AUC 0.8675 versus station memory AP 0.2782, AB411 AP 0.1779, and Virtual Beach-class MLR AP 0.3754.

sections.title: Decision use
sections.body: Supports retrospective triage, pilot design, and risk ranking in deployable counties.

sections.title: Boundary
sections.body: Do not claim 100 percent accuracy, safe-to-swim guidance, live deployment, or San Diego deployment calibration.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts, local comprehensive reports, and the current featured-findings ledger.
full_report.verdict: Calibrated HGBT ranks California marine FIB exceedances at AP 0.5099 vs station-memory 0.2782 (AUC 0.8675) on 89,321 held-out 2022+ beach-days; deployable statewide except San Diego.
full_report.scope: Question: does the bacteria model rank risky beach-days better than the simple rules a health department already has? On 89,321 forward-in-time California beach-days from 2022 onward (8,999 exceedances; trained on rows through 2019, calibrated on 2020-2021), the calibrated HGBT reaches average precision 0.5099 and AUC 0.8675, beating station memory (0.2782), a Virtual Beach-class regression (0.3754), and the AB411 rain rule (0.1779), with calibration error ECE 0.0087 in the deployable stratum and ~half of exceedances captured at a top-10% review budget (precision 0.5087, recall 0.5049). No AP confidence interval has been computed, so confidence rests on the large held-out sample and the low calibration error. Hard limits: this is a retrospective out-of-time test, not a live or prospective advisory feed; San Diego is excluded because its calibration breaks under a 2022 regime shift; and a fixed two-day reveal-lag proxy makes the headline about 0.02 AP optimistic (conservative available-at AP 0.4899). A county or research partner would use it to prioritize which beach-days to sample, review, or pilot-advise.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: We tested whether the MBAL bacteria model actually ranks risky beach-days better than simpler ways a health department could rank them: recent station history, AB411 rain rules, prior-lab history, month climatology, and a Virtual Beach-class regression baseline.

It does. On 89,321 held-out 2022+ beach-days with 8,999 exceedances, the calibrated HGBT reaches AP 0.5099 and AUC 0.8675. The important plain-language point is that this is not just memorizing dirty stations: it beats station memory by a large AP margin in the deployable statewide stratum.

The caveat is equally important: this is a retrospective operational-grade nowcast, not a live public advisory feed, and San Diego remains outside the deployment claim until its calibration problem is fixed.

sections.title: Adversarial Critic Read
body: The biggest ways to overclaim this result are to call it prospective, to merge San Diego into deployment language, or to hide the lab-result availability lag.
These are handled by the result's stated boundary: it is a retrospective forward-time test, San Diego is excluded, and the available-at AP is reported beside the headline AP.

findings.title: Clean-to-dirty beach surprise reframe passes the retrospective gate
findings.headline: AP 0.4177 vs station-memory 0.1990; +0.2187 AP; 2.10x lift
findings.plain: The model is especially useful when the beach was recently clean and then flips dirty. On this currently-clean slice, station memory is weak because yesterday being clean is part of the setup; the model still finds the risky flips.
findings.how: The target is reframed from ordinary exceedance nowcast to prior-clean onset and evaluated on 80,951 test rows with 6,022 events.
findings.note: Target reframe and retrospective gate. Prospective post-freeze lockbox scoring is still required before deployment language.

full_report.verdict: On recently-clean California beach-days, the model ranks clean-to-dirty exceedance onset at AP 0.4177 vs station-memory 0.199 (+0.2187 AP, 2.1x); retrospective, not yet prospectively scored.
full_report.scope: Question: can we catch beaches that look recently clean but are about to turn dirty? Restricting the held-out bacteria test frame to the 80,951 prior-clean beach-days (6,022 onset events, base rate 0.0744), the model ranks these surprise flips at average precision 0.4177 versus 0.199 for station memory — a +0.2187 AP gain (2.1x) — because simple persistence is uninformative by construction on a slice that starts clean. No confidence interval is filed and no operating threshold is set, so this stands as a ranking result, not a calibrated alarm. The hard limit is that this is a retrospective target reframe of the bacteria nowcast, not a prospective pilot, and the frozen lockbox has not yet been scored. Beach managers would use it for surprise-event triage: flagging recently clean beaches that routine station history would under-rank, before the next sample or advisory review.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This asks a sharper public-health question than the main nowcast: can we catch beaches that were recently clean and are about to turn dirty?
Station memory is weak on this slice because the setup already says the beach was clean. The model still ranks risky flips well: AP 0.4177 versus station-memory AP 0.1990.
That makes this useful as an early-warning/prioritization result, but it is still retrospective and should not be sold as a prospective pilot until the frozen lockbox is scored.

findings.title: Frozen bacteria advisory rule catches most exceedances, with false flags disclosed
findings.headline: Threshold 0.0635; recall 0.8645; precision 0.2301; 3.35 false flags per caught event
findings.plain: The frozen thresholded rule catches about 86 percent of exceedances in the held-out deployable stratum, but it is intentionally high-recall and noisy: most warnings are false positives.
findings.how: Threshold tau=0.0635 was selected on 2020-2021 validation for recall >=0.80 and evaluated on the 2022+ EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO test split.
findings.note: Retrospective operating point for pilot design and decision-curve review, not a public advisory feed.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts, local comprehensive reports, and the current featured-findings ledger.
full_report.verdict: At a calibrated probability cutoff of 0.0635 (fixed on 2020-21 validation), the rule catches recall 0.8645 of exceedances at precision 0.2301 on 89,321 held-out 2022+ beach-days — high recall, 3.35 false advisories per caught event.
full_report.scope: Question: where should ranking become an advisory-style rule, and at what cost? Flagging a beach when the calibrated exceedance probability is at least 0.0635 — a threshold chosen on 2020-2021 validation and never re-tuned on test — catches 7,780 held-out 2022+ exceedances and misses 1,219 (recall 0.8645) at precision 0.2301, while issuing 26,026 false flags, or 3.35 false advisories per caught exceedance. A Vickers-Elkin decision-curve analysis (valid because the model is calibrated, ECE<0.05) shows the calibrated model beats both 'advise everywhere' and 'advise nowhere' for policy cost ratios p_t from 0.02 to 0.30. The rule is deliberately high-recall and low-precision, and it is a frozen retrospective operating point for the EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO stratum, not a prospectively validated or public safe-to-swim alert (lockbox scoring pending). A health department or pilot team would use it to make the recall-versus-false-alarm tradeoff explicit when designing advisory policy.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This is the point where ranking becomes an advisory-style rule: issue a flag if calibrated exceedance probability is at least 0.0635.
It catches most true exceedances, but it is intentionally noisy. On the held-out test, it catches 7,780 exceedances and misses 1,219; it also issues 26,026 false flags.
That is useful for policy design because the tradeoff is visible. It is not a public advisory feed until governance and prospective validation are done.

sections.title: Decision Curve

tables.title: Net-benefit range
rows: Vickers-Elkin net benefit; valid because the model is calibrated on the retrospective out-of-time proxy (ECE<0.05); prospective lockbox pending
Model beats advise-all and advise-none for p_t

No fabricated dollar/illness counts. The trade-off is stated in operational units (exceedances caught vs false advisories) + the DCA-implied cost ratio. A site that wants fewer closures raises tau (toward the max-precision point); a site prioritising public health lowers it (toward max-detection). EPA recreational-water practice errs toward warning, i.e. a LOW tau, which this rule reflects.

sections.title: Critic Read
sections.body: The result is valuable because the threshold was selected before the test split and because the false-flag burden is disclosed. The thing to avoid is pretending high recall means high precision.

findings.title: Harmonic spring-neap tide range adds a small real bacteria signal
findings.headline: Base AP 0.5008; delta AP +0.0091; CI [0.0044, 0.0140]; perm p=0.0196

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts, local comprehensive reports, and the current featured-findings ledger.
full_report.verdict: PASS - harmonic spring-neap tide range is a small but real driver for bacteria ranking: +0.0091 AP over a 0.5008 base (bootstrap CI [0.0044, 0.014], permutation p=0.0196). Keep it as a feature; do not build a tide-only model or call it a cause.
full_report.scope: Question: does the spring-neap tidal-range cycle help rank which beach-days exceed bacteria thresholds? Adding harmonic tide-range terms to the bacteria model lifts average precision by +0.0091 AP over a 0.5008 base, and the gain holds up: a station-cluster bootstrap puts the 95% CI at [0.0044, 0.014] (above zero), and a shuffle-and-refit permutation null gives p=0.0196 against a null mean delta of -0.0018. The hard limit is size and interpretation - the effect is small, it is not a standalone forecast, and it is not causal proof. Practical use: keep harmonic tide range as a retained physics driver in the operational bacteria feature set.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: Earlier write-ups of this result described a missing-data gap. That gap is now closed.
The actual result is modest but real: harmonic spring-neap tide range adds +0.0091 AP and survives both bootstrap and shuffle-null checks.
This is worth keeping as a driver, but not worth turning into a separate model or a large feature program.

full_report.verdict: PASS and PROMOTED - CatBoost is the new production champion behind the bacteria nowcast: AP 0.507 vs the retired HGBT's 0.4938 (+0.0132 AP, paired bootstrap CI [0.010, 0.0164] excludes zero), with better calibration (ECE 0.0095 vs 0.0174). A small, reliable hardening upgrade, not a breakthrough.
full_report.scope: Question: does swapping CatBoost in for the previous HGBT model rank risky beach-days better? On the same 89,321-row EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO held-out frame (base rate 0.1007), paired on identical rows, CatBoost scores AP 0.507 against HGBT's 0.4938 - a +0.0132 AP gain whose paired bootstrap CI [0.010, 0.0164] stays above zero. It also wins 13 of 15 leave-one-county-out folds and calibrates better (ECE 0.0095 vs 0.0174). On that basis CatBoost was promoted to production champion and HGBT-isotonic was retired (LightGBM stays a benchmark only). The margin is genuinely small and incremental: the causal feature design and calibration carry the result, the learner is a refinement on top. Practical use: it is the deployed ranking engine behind the bacteria nowcast, inheriting that nowcast's caveats (San Diego excluded, prospective lockbox pending).

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: We asked whether swapping the model behind the bacteria nowcast for CatBoost actually ranks risky beach-days better than the previous HGBT model, on the exact same held-out beach-days.
It does, by a small, paired-verified margin: AP 0.507 vs 0.4938 (+0.0132), with a bootstrap confidence interval that stays above zero, and lower calibration error (0.0095 vs 0.0174).

CatBoost was promoted to production champion on this basis. The honest framing is incremental hardening, not a breakthrough - the science (causal feature design, calibrated thresholds) is what carries the result; the learner is a refinement on top of it.

findings.title: Physics-pi zero-shot bacteria screen works as label-free cold-start ranking
findings.headline: Mean AP 0.1511 vs base 0.0909; 12/12 targets beat source-stratum null
findings.plain: Hand-built physics-pi coordinates support label-free zero-shot FIB ranking across held-out domains. This is a cold-start ranking screen for places without local labels, not a calibrated advisory probability model.

full_report.verdict: PASS as a cold-start ranking screen - a label-free physics-coordinate model transfers to 12 held-out domains (mean AP 0.1511 vs base 0.0909, domain-bootstrap AP-base CI [0.0365, 0.086], 12/12 beat the null). full_report.scope: Question: can a label-free physics-coordinate model rank beach bacteria risk in new places that have no local labels yet? In a 708,337-row leave-one-domain-out screen spanning 12 domains and 57,628 events, the pi-plus-logistic model averages AP 0.1511 against a 0.0909 mean base rate, with a domain-bootstrap AP-base CI of [0.0365, 0.086], and all 12 targets beat their source-stratum null. The hard limit: it is uncalibrated, and it does not beat local history. A separate ~764k-row leave-one-basin-out comparison that does include local station memory finds memory stronger (AUC 0.7637 vs pi 0.6124, pi ahead on 0/12 coasts) - a different experiment from the 708k-row zero-shot screen. Practical use: cold-start triage and ranking for a new jurisdiction or sparse monitoring program before enough local labels exist to train a mature local model.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This asks whether a simple physics-coordinate language can travel to places where we do not have local labels yet.
The answer is yes for ranking: across 12 held-out domains, pi+logistic averages AP 0.1511 versus base 0.0909, and every target beats the source-stratum null.
The answer is not yes for calibrated public advisories. When local memory exists, local memory is stronger; pi is a cold-start screen and a residual physics layer.

sections.title: What Pi Is
body: The pi coordinates are label-free process proxies: rain loading, UV/thermal decay opportunity, delivered UV dose, wind transport, and flushing.
They are computed from weather/location/date inputs and do not use bacteria labels or concentration values. Labels enter only when training/evaluating the downstream model.

sections.title: Zero-Shot Evidence Outside California
sections.body: Each target domain is held out, a logistic model is trained outside it, and the held-out target is scored with the same pi coordinate axes.

sections.title: Why It Matters
sections.body: The same coordinate meanings can be used in every geography. That makes this a practical onboarding/ranking layer for a place that lacks enough local monitoring history to train a mature local model.

sections.title: Critic Read
sections.body: The top risks are overclaiming calibration, claiming pi beats mature local memory, or promoting learned tensor/foundation-model variants over the raw pi control. The evidence does not support those claims.

findings.title: Physics-pi plus local memory improves station-held-out bacteria ranking
findings.headline: HGBT hybrid AP 0.6761 vs memory 0.6494; delta +0.0267 CI [0.0214, 0.0326]
findings.plain: Inside California, pi coordinates add residual ranking value when combined with local memory. The row-alignment shuffle critic confirms the gain depends on correctly aligned pi values.
findings.how: Three-fold GroupKFold by station/group over 475,669 rows, 59,710 events, 824 groups, and 17 counties. Inputs are 36 memory/history features plus 5 pi coordinates.
findings.note: Ranking candidate only; county-held-out calibration fails the deployable-probability gate.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts, local comprehensive reports, and the current featured-findings ledger.
full_report.verdict: PASS as a ranking upgrade, GATED for deployment: adding 5 label-free physics-weather coordinates to 36 station-memory features lifts exceedance ranking (HGBT AP 0.6761 vs 0.6494, +0.0267), but the gain does not survive the county-held-out deployment check.
full_report.scope: When a beach already has its own sampling history, do label-free physics-weather coordinates still add predictive signal for marine Enterococcus/FIB exceedance? On station-held-out rows (824 site groups, 475,669 rows, 59,710 events), an HGBT model combining 36 memory features with 5 pi coordinates ranks exceedance days at AP 0.6761 versus 0.6494 for memory alone, a +0.0267 gain whose 95% CI [0.0214, 0.0326] excludes zero and survives a pi-shuffle negative control. At a top-10% triage budget it captures 53.9% of events at 67.7% precision. Hard limit: the lift fails the strict county-held-out deployment check (county delta AP CI [-0.0011, 0.0555] crosses zero), so it is a ranking improvement, not a deploy-ready probability model. Use it to sharpen triage where local history already exists but residual site-day signal remains.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This tests the exact question that matters after the zero-shot result: if local station history already exists, do the label-free pi physics coordinates still add anything?
Yes for ranking. The best direct HGBT hybrid uses 36 memory features plus 5 pi coordinates and scores AP 0.6761 versus HGBT memory AP 0.6494 on station-held-out rows.

No for deployment probabilities yet. The forward-time calibration gate passes, but the county-held-out deployment gate fails the strict rule, so this stays a ranking candidate rather than a deploy-ready probability model.

full_report.scope: Can we flag a toxic domoic-acid bloom one visit ahead, especially right after a clean reading? On the slice that matters most - cells whose last sample was clean, where simple persistence is uninformative - the forecast ranks onset at AP 0.1347 versus 0.0259 for the strongest fair baseline (seasonal-naive), a +0.1089 gain with 95% CI [0.0566, 0.2078] and permutation p=0.002, and it beats seasonal-naive in all 8 leave-one-station checks. The honest headline is this prior-clean onset slice; the broader pooled-versus-persistence comparison is supportive context, not the claim, because its margin is not separated from zero. Hard limit: only 34 onset events, a retrospective holdout, and no deployment threshold. Use it to prioritize HAB follow-up sampling when recent readings look clean but onset risk is elevated.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This result asks whether we can warn on a toxic domoic-acid onset before the next visit, especially when the last observation was clean.
The answer is yes in the scoped retrospective gate: AP 0.1347 versus the best nondegenerate baseline AP 0.0259, with CI excluding zero and permutation p=0.002.
The event count is small (34 onset events), so the result is valuable but should stay scoped and prospective validation should come next.

findings.title: mcyE genotype-to-toxin effect definitively replicates in Alberta
findings.headline: Partial rho 0.6824; CI [0.6500, 0.7150]; 1,461 samples; 55 lakes
findings.plain: In Alberta open-data cyanobacteria monitoring, toxic genotype mcyE explains microcystin beyond biomass in the detectable/bloom regime. The effect is large and stable across lakes and years.
findings.how: Partial-rank association conditional on biomass, with permutation and bootstrap checks plus leave-one-lake and leave-one-year robustness.
findings.note: Association among detectable samples, not a forward bloom forecast or toxin warning by itself.

full_report.verdict: PASS - the toxin-production gene mcyE tracks measured microcystin even after controlling for biomass and cell counts (partial rho 0.6824) across 55 Alberta lakes; a replicated biological association, not a toxin forecast.
full_report.scope: Does the toxin-production gene mcyE still track measured microcystin once you account for how much cyanobacterial biomass and how many cells are present? In 55 Alberta lakes (1,461 samples, 2012-2016) it does, strongly: the partial Spearman correlation of total mcyE with microcystin, conditioned on biomass/cells, is 0.6824 (bootstrap CI [0.65, 0.715]; permutation p <1/Nperm, with Nperm unrecorded), and it stays positive in every leave-one-lake-out (49 folds) and leave-one-year-out check. The estimate is conditioned on detectable, bloom-regime samples and dilutes on the full population that includes non-detects (no bloom -> no toxin -> no genes), so this is a replicated biological association, not a calibrated cyanotoxin forecast or a deployed lake-warning tool. Its value: it justifies keeping molecular genotype measurements in cyanotoxin-risk research and prioritizing datasets where mcyE, biomass, and toxin are measured together.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This is not a forecasting model. It tests a biological relationship: does the toxin-production gene signal track microcystin after accounting for biomass/cells?
In Alberta, yes, strongly. The total mcyE effect has partial rho 0.6824 with a tight bootstrap CI [0.650, 0.715], and the Microcystis-specific test is similarly strong.
That makes this a clean replication of the genotype-to-toxin relationship, not a deployed lake-warning product.

full_report.verdict: PASS, scoped - the Alberta mcyE-to-toxin link replicates outside Alberta for absolute mcyE after biomass control (partial rho 0.4423); the ideal fraction-with-biomass version remains too data-starved to test.
full_report.scope: Does the Alberta genotype-to-toxin signal hold beyond Alberta? In a national corpus (60 samples, 12 stations), the scoped answer is yes for absolute mcyE after controlling for biomass: partial Spearman rho 0.4423 (bootstrap CI [0.193, 0.646]; permutation p=0.0024), sign-stable across all leave-one-station-out (12/12) and leave-one-year-out (6/6) folds. The more biologically ideal test - the fraction of cyanobacteria carrying mcyE, conditioned on biomass - cannot be established here: only 5 rows have all the needed measurements, so it is underpowered/untestable, and the site-controlled fraction test without biomass fails (rho 0.2303, p=0.1186). Read this as evidence the molecular signal is not just an Alberta artifact, and as a reason to collect complete mcyE-fraction + biomass + toxin panels; do not claim the ideal fraction relationship has replicated nationally.

findings.title: Cyano onset has a scoped cold-start phenology prior
findings.headline: Leave-one-state lat-quad skill 0.2018; CI [0.0442, 0.3151]
findings.plain: Latitude/quadratic geography is a real cold-start prior for lake cyanobacteria onset timing. It helps decide when to start looking at cold-start lakes, but it is not an abundance, toxin, or bloom-severity model.
findings.how: Red-team checks evaluate held-out state, lat-band, year, and excluding-2026 variants. Winter meteorology can harden the prior, but safe wording anchors on lat/quadratic held-out skill.
findings.note: Claim onset timing only. Do not sell photoperiod-only mechanism or toxin prediction.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts, local analysis reports.
full_report.verdict: PASS, scoped - the date of the first cyano bloom is partly predictable before a lake has local history, from a geography-PLUS-winter-weather law (leave-one-state skill 0.2018; combined geography+winter skill 0.2687), not from latitude or photoperiod alone.
full_report.scope: Can you predict when the first high/very-high CyAN bloom of the year arrives at a lake with no local monitoring history? Across 13,131 lake-years (2,181 lakes, 47 US states), partly yes. A latitude/quadratic geography term gives a keyless cold-start skill of 0.2018 over a global-mean baseline (leave-one-state CV, CI [0.0442, 0.3151]), holding up under leave-one-latband and leave-one-year splits and after excluding partial-2026 rows (0.2005). Prior-winter meteorology alone is comparable-to-stronger (skill 0.2647), and combining geography with winter weather reaches 0.2687 - so the defensible claim is a geography-plus-winter-weather onset law, not pure latitude and not photoperiod, which is too weak to anchor. The signal forecasts sampling-season timing only: use it to decide when to begin cyano sampling or remote-sensing attention at unmonitored lakes. It says nothing about bloom abundance, persistence, or toxin concentration, and is not validated outside US CyAN lakes.

house.read: PASS but scoped: leave-one-state latitude/quadratic skill 0.2018, CI [0.0442, 0.3151].
Leave-one-state, leave-one-latband, and leave-one-year tests over lake-year onset timing.
Regression / onset timing skill score.
Not applicable; timing prior, not event probability.
Cold-start planning for when to start cyano sampling or remote-sensing attention at lakes without local history.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This result says the first high/very-high CyAN bloom onset date is partly predictable before a lake has local history.

The useful signal is not photoperiod alone. Two parts: latitude/quadratic geography is the keyless cold-start floor (skill 0.202, no weather inputs needed), and prior-winter meteorology alone is comparable-to-stronger (skill 0.2647); combined they reach 0.2687. The publishable claim is a geography-plus-winter-weather onset law, not pure latitude.
The result is intentionally scoped: it helps plan sampling season timing, not bloom abundance, persistence, or toxin concentration.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts and comprehensive local result reports.
full_report.verdict: Low-oxygen stress can be screened from non-oxygen water-quality covariates: AP 0.2922 vs 0.2427 baseline (+0.0496, CI [0.0379, 0.0646], excludes zero), N=15,408 held-out station-days. The tensor CP factor is not promoted (CI [-0.0054, 0.016] crosses zero).
full_report.scope: Can ordinary water-quality measurements, temperature, salinity, turbidity, conductance, and pH, flag low-oxygen stress at monitoring stations that lack a dissolved-oxygen sensor? On forward-in-time station-days (train through 2018, test from 2019; 15,408 test rows, 1,453 low-oxygen events, base rate 0.0943), these non-oxygen covariates rank risk at AP 0.2922 versus 0.2427 for a station-type/month/geography baseline, a +0.0496 gain whose 95% CI [0.0379, 0.0646] and permutation p=0.01 both exclude zero, with better calibration (Brier 0.1705 vs 0.1799). A tensor CP factor was also fit but is not promoted: its +0.0056 gain has CI [-0.0054, 0.016], which crosses zero. The practical use is a triage layer that prioritizes which under-instrumented stations are most likely under oxygen stress, never a substitute for a direct DO measurement, which must stay out of the inputs.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts and comprehensive local result reports.
full_report.verdict: Tokyo Bay bottom-water low-oxygen onset is forecastable 1-3 days ahead and no further: capture@20% beats the seasonal-naive baseline with CI excluding zero at 1, 2, and 3 days, but at 5+ days the CI crosses zero. Short-lead triage only.
full_report.scope: Can we forecast the onset of bottom-water hypoxia (bottom DO_min <= 2.0 mg/L) in Tokyo Bay, stations currently non-hypoxic that cross into hypoxia within the next h days, using only data available at forecast time? Across a forward-time horizon sweep (4 daily stations, 2010-2026; features at time t only, no forecast inputs), a ranking model beats the seasonal-naive baseline on capture@20% at 1 day (+0.1196, CI [0.0667, 0.2489]), 2 days (+0.0915, CI [0.0233, 0.2261]), and 3 days (+0.0877, CI [0.0287, 0.2022]); model AP rises from 0.4771 at h=1 to 0.6946 at h=3. At 5 days and beyond the capture@20% gain's CI crosses zero, so the forecast is honest only out to a 3-day lead. The practical use is short-lead sampling and response prioritization, deciding which stations to check in the next few days, not multi-week hypoxia forecasting.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This result is useful because it tells us the real lead-time boundary, not just that a model can rank hypoxia states.
For Tokyo Bay low-DO onset, 1-3 day lead has supported capture@20% lift. At 5 days and beyond, the confidence intervals cross zero, so the report stops there.
That makes the finding operationally honest: short-lead triage, not long-range hypoxia forecasting.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved evidence artifacts, local comprehensive reports, and the current featured-findings ledger.
full_report.verdict: PASS (retrospective): a leakage-guarded model forecasts particulate domoic-acid exceedance better than persistence (AP 0.2571 vs 0.1415, +0.1156, CI [0.0468, 0.265], perm p=0.005); 51 events, not deployment-ready.
full_report.scope: Can a strict, leakage-guarded model forecast particulate domoic-acid (pDA) exceedance better than persistence and seasonality? On a chronological holdout after 2021-05-10 (1,705 test rows, 51 events, base rate 0.0299), it ranks exceedance at AP 0.2571 versus 0.1415 for the best baseline (persistence), a +0.1156 gain with 95% CI [0.0468, 0.265] and permutation p=0.005, AUC 0.8604. The strict framing matters: an earlier permissive probe scored AP 0.997 by leaking same-sample pDA-derived fields, which are excluded here. Hard limit: retrospective only, small N (51 events), and no prospective replay or leave-station stress test yet, so this is not deployment-ready. Use it as a triage signal to prioritize HAB sampling and review, not to replace independent sampling.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: This result asks whether a strict, leakage-guarded HAB model can predict particulate domoic-acid exceedance better than simple persistence and seasonality.
It passes on the chronological holdout: AP 0.2571 versus persistence AP 0.1415, with CI excluding zero and permutation p=0.005.
The strict card matters because an earlier generic probe was too permissive. This one excludes current same-sample pDA-derived fields and keeps the deployment caveat visible.

findings.title: Unified label-free physics interlingua clears a 12-domain zero-shot gate
findings.headline: pi+logistic mean AP 0.1511 · mean lift 1.7524x over base
findings.plain: Hand-built non-dimensional physics groups (pi) plus a simple logistic model rank beach-bacteria exceedance across 12 held-out domains with no local labels, beating the matched base rate on every domain. This is the positive transfer result behind the earlier interlingua boundary nulls.
findings.how: Leave-one-domain-out zero-shot over 708,337 held-out rows / 57,628 events; within-source-stratum permutation null (200 iters), station-clustered AP-minus-base CI, and a held-out-domain bootstrap. CARTE string models are not a dependency and do not beat the same-sample numeric baselines.
findings.note: Conservative cross-region transfer baseline and feature-contract target; a ranking/triage screen, not a calibrated probability or a deployed advisory.

full_report.generated: Generated 2026-06-26 from saved zero-shot transfer artifacts and local analysis reports.
full_report.verdict: PASS as a cross-domain ranking screen: one label-free physics encoding (pi) plus a simple logistic model ranks beach-bacteria exceedance in 12 held-out regions with no local labels, mean AP 0.1511 vs base rate 0.0909, beating a within-region permutation null in 12/12 regions.
full_report.scope: Can a single set of non-dimensional physics groups (a 'physics interlingua') rank coastal-bacteria risk in a region the model never trained on, using only weather/geography and no local labels? Across 12 held-out domains (708,337 rows, 57,628 exceedance events), a pi-plus-logistic model reaches mean average precision 0.1511 against a mean base rate of 0.0909 - a mean 1.75x lift (minimum 1.19x), with the held-out-domain bootstrap putting mean AP at [0.1053, 0.2128] and mean AP-minus-base at [0.0368, 0.0865], above zero. Every one of the 12 domains beats its own within-source-stratum permutation null at the 95th percentile and has a station-clustered AP-minus-base interval above zero. This is a transfer/triage screen for places without local monitoring history, not a calibrated probability or an advisory; string-based table foundation models (CARTE) are not needed and do not beat the same-sample numeric baselines.

house.read: PASS as a label-free cross-domain ranking screen: pi+logistic mean AP 0.1511 vs base 0.0909 (mean lift 1.75x), 12/12 domains above their permutation null.
Entire domain/region held out (leave-one-domain-out); 708,337 rows, 57,628 events across 12 regions; no target-region labels or local history used.
Zero-shot ranking / triage screen, evaluated by AP vs matched base rate.
Not calibrated for advisory probability anywhere; the claim is ranking only.
Cold-start risk ranking for a coast or region that has no local monitoring history yet, as an onboarding/triage layer.

sections.title: Plain English Bottom Line
body: The question is whether one shared way of describing the physics of a coastline - turned into a few dimensionless numbers from weather and geography - lets a model rank where bacteria exceedances are likely in a region it has never seen, with no local lab data.

It does, for ranking: across 12 held-out regions the encoding plus a simple logistic model averages AP 0.1511 against a base rate of 0.0909 (mean 1.75x, never below 1.19x), and every region clears a within-region permutation null.

The honest bound is that this ranks risk; it does not produce trustworthy probabilities for a new jurisdiction, and where a region already has its own monitoring history that local history is the stronger signal. The value is the cold start - a useful first ranking before local labels exist.

sections.title: Per-Domain Results
sections.body: Each region is held out, the model is trained on the others, and the held-out region is scored on the shared physics axes. The lift is the ratio of AP to that region's base rate.

sections.title: How Sure
sections.body: Two independent checks support the aggregate: a within-source-stratum permutation null (200 iterations) that all 12 domains beat at the 95th percentile, and a held-out-domain bootstrap.

sections.title: Critic Read
sections.body: The risks are calling a ranking screen a calibrated model, claiming the encoding beats mature local memory, or leaning on a heavier model than the data needs.

tables.title: Overclaim checks
rows: No local calibration or probability deployment anywhere.
Where local monitoring history exists it is the stronger ranker; this is for the cold start.
String-table CARTE models are not required and do not beat the same-sample numeric baselines.
This is the cross-domain view of the same gauntlet that the marine-FIB cold-start screen reports; they are one result, not two.

sections.title: What This Is Good For
sections.body: Use it to rank risk and prioritize where to start monitoring in a region with no local history. Once enough local labels accumulate, compare against local memory and calibrate before any operational use.

sections.title: Hard Boundaries
sections.body: Not a safe-to-swim model, not a calibrated probability, and not evidence that the encoding replaces local history. Within-region, it is a label-free cold-start ranking screen.

sections.title: Suggested Publication Venue
sections.body: Best fit: Frontiers in Marine Science, or Limnology and Oceanography: Methods. A generalizable, label-free transfer method of methodological interest. Frame carefully as a computational public-data transfer/ranking result across domains, not a calibrated advisory model.

gates.evidence: Mean AP 0.1511 beats mean base 0.0909; 12/12 domains beat base and their permutation null.
Leave-one-domain-out; no target-region labels or local history in the score.
Within-source-stratum permutation null; anti-leak rule enforced in the run config.
Domain-bootstrap mean AP-minus-base CI [0.0368, 0.0865] excludes zero; 12/12 beat null p95.
Ranking only; no calibrated advisory probability claim.
No clean-clone rerun filed for this run.
Headline is mean AP 0.1511 over 12 held-out domains.
CARTE-not-needed and beats-local-memory overclaims explicitly blocked.

findings.title: Australia physics+memory combo beats local memory for FIB triage
findings.headline: combo AP 0.235 vs memory 0.0805 · dAP +0.1545
findings.plain: On New South Wales beach FIB, transferred physics groups added to local memory beat memory alone in a forward time split, and the combo is well calibrated. Pure physics transfer alone does not beat memory; the confirmed win is the augmentation.
findings.how: Forward split (fit on early half, calibrate on first 40% of late half, evaluate on last 60%); paired CI clean per australia_transfer_confirm.py.

metrics.held_out: forward split: fit<=median date; calibrate=first 40% of late half; eval=last 60%

findings.note: Scoped onset-RANKING/triage aid for monitored AU beaches; NOT a public-health advisory. Low-N. Confirmed by australia_transfer_confirm.py (combo>memory, paired CI clean).

findings.title: Cyano season-ahead watchlist is a shipped keyless allocator
findings.headline: 337 lakes flagged of 1686 · 5-20wk lead
findings.plain: A keyless recurrence-plus-season allocator ranks currently-clean lakes for cyanobacteria bloom risk weeks ahead, catching more new blooms than status-quo lake-memory ranking under the same sampling budget.
findings.how: Capture-at-budget replay over national clean-lake lake-years; lake-recurrence x target-week-of-year carrier only (no weather forecast).
findings.note: ~5-7 pts more new blooms caught vs status-quo lake-memory at 5-20wk lead; recurrence+season only; multi-week gain is broadly distributed across states

findings.title: Florida red-tide season-ahead watchlist (FL-only) is shipped
findings.headline: 371 sites flagged of 1855 · ~+0.20-0.23 onset capture
findings.plain: A Florida-scoped keyless allocator ranks currently-clean sites for Karenia red-tide onset risk, catching ~0.20-0.23 more onset events than site-history ranking at a 20% sampling budget.
findings.how: Capture-at-budget replay over Florida clean sites; site-recurrence x target-week-of-year seasonal carrier (a seasonal calendar, not a weather forecast).
findings.note: ~+0.20-0.23 more onset events caught vs site-history ranking at 20% budget, 1-40wk out, Florida-scoped, seasonal-recurrence (not weather forecast)

findings.title: June-24 re-gate: surviving cards are honest rankers, not calibrated products
findings.headline: 9 deltas reproduced; 4 flat-prior NO-GO; 1 single-cluster REJECT
findings.plain: A senior re-gate reran every June-24 candidate with station-clustered bootstraps and a 2,000-iteration permutation null. The lifts are real and reproduce, but the decision-facing cards have poor calibration (high ECE), so they are ranking screens that must be recalibrated before any probability is shown. Shark and cetacean cards beat only a flat global prior and are explicit NO-GO; an imputed-DA card with a single test cluster is rejected.
findings.how: Each banked June-24 claim card was rerun through the runner's exact pipeline: the point delta was reproduced first, then re-gated with a station/site-clustered bootstrap CI, a 2,000-iteration label-shuffle permutation null, and ECE/Brier calibration.
findings.note: Keepers for the brief are the FIB station-held-out ranking screens and the HABMAP pDA proxy lead; recalibrate before showing probabilities, and do not promote the flat-prior shark/cetacean cards.

findings.headline: median AP 0.5222
findings.how: Gradient-boosted trees on weather/ocean/discharge/tide drivers + lab history. Honest headline = leave-one-county-out median Average Precision; San Diego is excluded because its huge volume and ~60% event rate flatter the pooled number.
findings.note: The pooled numbers below are NOT the primary headline. Pooled all-county AP/ROC-AUC are dominated by San Diego (large volume, ~60% base rate, AP~0.96) and overstate generalization. The honest operational headline is the leave-one-county-out result in reproduce/expected/spatial_holdout.json.
findings.plain: Predicts which beach-days will exceed safe bacteria limits, trained on every county but San Diego and tested on held-out counties — so the score reflects a NEW beach, not memorised history. It beats California's deployed rain-advisory rule in 9/9 counties.
findings.title: Beach bacteria forecast beats the deployed rule (leave-one-county-out)

findings.headline: AP 0.8782 · ROC-AUC 0.9362
findings.how: Random-forest / HGBT on 39 driver features; single train/valid/test time split.
findings.note: statewide_with_rainfall AP/ROC-AUC are the pooled, San-Diego-dominated cut; report the leave-one-county-out figures instead.
findings.plain: The pooled, all-California view: of ~62k held-out beach-days the model ranks risky days far above the base rate. This number is optimistic because San Diego dominates it — use the leave-one-county-out finding for the honest figure.
findings.title: Statewide + rainfall (pooled test split)

findings.headline: AP 0.841 vs 0.8782 full
findings.how: Same model, feature set cut from 39 to 17 (no lab-history).
findings.note: A pure-persistence model would collapse here; it doesn't.
findings.plain: Removing the recent-lab-result features only drops AP from 0.8782 to 0.841 — so the model is doing real driver-based work, not just echoing the last test.
findings.title: Persistence ablation (drop lab-history features)

findings.headline: AP 0.0838 · ROC 0.6206 (n_test 691)
findings.how: Same features, Monterey subset only.
findings.note: Negative result kept on purpose — it justifies the statewide corpus.
findings.plain: Trained and tested on Monterey alone there are only ~24 events in the test set — too few to learn from, so the model is near-useless here. This is WHY the lab pools statewide data.
findings.title: Monterey-only is too sparse to forecast

git.last_commit: ec75ece 2026-06-20T10:00:07-07:00 fix(cloud-sync): repair broken nightly MBAL-Cloud-Sync backup before big run

lab_memory.claim: Build one shared model of the coastal ocean — how its physics, weather, runoff, waves, tides, and life connect — so it can warn of rare water-quality events before they are measured directly.
Look for second- and third-order signal combinations across moorings, satellite, flow and weather data, rather than generic regression on raw sensors.
We reframed the bacteria problem around clean-to-dirty surprises, so the science targets events people cannot infer from yesterday's status alone. (See the Findings tab for the measured result.)

When we test whether a model carries over to a new region, we log the outcome with its baseline and permutation-null checks rather than asserting success — the regional-transfer story has been softened by tougher held-out audits.

Across many tested inputs, external rainfall is the main clear driver of beach-bacteria risk; most other candidate drivers — including waves and tide level — wash out against a full baseline. We call this the driver-null pattern.

The current strongest new claims are intentionally scoped: domoic acid is promoted on prior-clean toxic-onset rows, cyanobacteria is promoted as an onset-timing prior, and warning-screen work is not treated as a deployed alert system.
The strongest evidence is a combination — honest baselines, held-out tests, calibration, and failure analysis — not any single score.
Null results are kept on the record beside the wins, so the next experiment starts from the truth instead of a forgotten dead end.
Broad station coverage tends to matter more than long history at a single site when the goal is a statewide model.

The latest data acquisition work separates usable surface inputs from blocked global rollout inputs: MERRA-2 surface fields are available through Earth Engine for station-level features, Sentinel-3 chlorophyll source data is present locally, and full global Prithvi pressure-level rollout still needs the NASA GES DISC agreement.

The semantic-shadow lakehouse is now useful as a question generator across bacteria, HAB, and whale targets, but its correlations and temporal-holdout lifts remain discovery leads until a reproduction job turns them into evidence.
Time-series foundation models repeatedly failed to beat a simple persistence baseline on the mooring data, so they were pruned rather than promoted.
Automatic model self-repair when training loss spikes was rejected: a loss spike can be a real ocean anomaly, so the system may flag it but must not silently rewrite itself.
The open frontier is prospective scoring — testing new observations after the freeze date before calling a retrospective result final.
Can driver-aware neural models overcome the modest or negative skill seen in early multi-driver forecasting runs?

Can the acquired Prithvi and Granite source adapters create reusable coastal features that beat existing tabular baselines, once they are evaluated under the same held-out gates as the current registry?

vocabulary.label: Source dataset
vocabulary.what: Raw, joinable evidence we collect — coverage, rows, recency, provenance. Not a model.

vocabulary.label: Target / label
vocabulary.what: The thing being explained or predicted — it defines the scientific question.

vocabulary.label: Signal / feature
vocabulary.what: A candidate explanatory input, tested through gates until it earns KEEP, WASH, or REJECT.

vocabulary.label: Representation
vocabulary.what: A transformed feature space used to expose hidden structure or cross-domain transfer.

vocabulary.label: Diagnostic / lens
vocabulary.what: A way to look for structure. A discovery aid, not a model.

vocabulary.label: Learner / discovery engine
vocabulary.what: An algorithm used to search for relationships. Before it is fitted to data, this is NOT a trained model.

vocabulary.label: Trained model card
vocabulary.what: A fitted, evaluated, registered learner plus its evidence and status. Can be claimable, benchmark, or null.

vocabulary.label: Baseline / null
vocabulary.what: An honest comparator that prevents fake discoveries. A baseline is not a discovery claim.

vocabulary.label: Finding
vocabulary.what: A promoted scientific statement — only after leakage, baseline, and bootstrap/null checks pass.
examples: driver-null law (rainfall is the main clear external driver)
spring–neap lift is real but does not generalize spatially
soil moisture is redundant with discharge

targets.label: Beach bacteria exceedance
targets.question: Will a beach exceed the AB411 enterococcus standard (>104 MPN/100 mL)?
targets.plain: Our strongest result: a statewide bacteria nowcast at AP 0.5099 / ROC-AUC 0.8675, calibrated in the deployable EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO stratum, with clean-to-dirty onset, harmonic tide, sampling-triage, and pi+memory ranking evidence. Prospective lockbox scoring is still pending, and San Diego calibration is excluded from deployment language.

targets.label: Domoic-acid shellfish toxin
targets.question: Will a pier's next sample exceed particulate domoic acid 500 ng/L?
targets.plain: Supported, with scope discipline: the strict-critic HAB pDA exceedance card reaches AP 0.2571 vs persistence AP 0.1415, dAP +0.1156 with CI [0.0468, 0.265] and permutation-p 0.005. The prior-clean toxic-onset slice also clears: AP 0.1347 versus seasonal-naive AP 0.0259, margin +0.1089 with station-clustered CI [0.0566, 0.2078] and permutation-p 0.002. These are retrospective held-out claims, not deployed warnings.

targets.label: Cross-country zero-shot transfer
targets.question: Can a label-free model rank bacteria exceedance in a country with no local training labels?
targets.plain: Real but scoped: physics-pi supports label-free zero-shot bacteria ranking across held-out FIB domains, but pi alone does not replace mature local memory and broad cross-hazard pi sweeps are null. Use as cold-start ranking, not calibrated advisory probability.

targets.label: Coastal hypoxia (low oxygen) lead
targets.question: Will bottom water go hypoxic (DO ≤ 2 mg/L) 7–14 days ahead?
targets.plain: The low-oxygen lane is mixed. Tokyo Bay onset forecasting is useful only at short 1-3 day lead, WQP oxygen-stress screening clears a forward station-day gate, and GRU state-target dynamics are a scoped research signal. The Gulf hypoxia grid-cell flag is a no-go once effort and cell-memory controls are applied.
targets.detail: Safe wording: scoped short-lead/onset and oxygen-stress screening evidence, plus visible no-go boundaries. Do not claim Gulf Dead Zone early warning, cross-basin solved hypoxia, or 14-28 day onset warning from the current artifacts.

targets.label: Fjord oxygen-renewal onset
targets.question: Will a dense-water renewal re-oxygenate an anoxic fjord within ~3 weeks?
targets.plain: Deep-water density gives +0.08 AP of renewal-onset lead-skill that is year-robust at ~21-day lead — the slow-driver principle partially transfers to a second basin once the carrier and objective match the physics.

targets.label: Lake cyanobacteria bloom onset
targets.question: When will a lake's cyanobacteria bloom season start?
targets.plain: Claimable but scoped: latitude/quadratic geography is a real cold-start bloom-onset timing prior under held-out state, lat-band, year, and red-team checks. The claim is onset timing and sampling-start prior, not abundance prediction; photoperiod-only is not the anchor, and daily meteorology/interlingua transfer remains null.

targets.label: Whale (cetacean) mortality
targets.question: Do prior ocean drivers predict California whale mortality beyond seasonal climatology?
targets.plain: We reconstructed the 2019–2023 gray-whale die-off from citizen-science (iNaturalist) records and tested prey-collapse, climate, and domoic-acid pathways. Honest result: the prey-collapse link (krill, rockfish) is SUGGESTIVE but NOT robust — it fails a 17-year cluster bootstrap — and the domoic-acid→death link does not hold. The ecology/mortality lane is largely signal-poor; we keep it on the record as an open frontier, not a win.

targets.label: Sea-star wasting / kelp ecosystem
targets.question: Can ocean drivers explain sea-star wasting and kelp-forest ecosystem shifts?
targets.plain: An exploratory cross-dataset lane in the ecology/mortality family; like whale mortality, the early ecology correlations have been hard to make survive strict cross-validation.

targets.label: SF Bay compound coastal flood
targets.question: Will SF Bay see an extreme still-water-level (coastal-flood) day, and is it compound (rain + tide/surge)?
targets.plain: A driver-lift wash: the free harmonic tide table alone is the best naive predictor (AP 0.223 / AUROC 0.945); adding weather/runoff drivers (AP 0.294) is NOT CI-separated from tide-only, so there is no demonstrated driver lift yet.

targets.label: ddPCR-positive culture-safe days
targets.question: Are same-day ddPCR positives among culture-safe beach-days a real early warning target?
targets.plain: No clean target yet. In San Diego, ddPCR-positive/culture-safe days have raw forward exceedance lift, but that lift largely disappears after full culture-history controls: ddPCR adds only +0.0147 AUC with CI crossing zero. Treat the raw effect as persistence/history structure, not a promoted molecular early-warning claim.

targets.label: Tsunami half-duration warning screen
targets.question: Can rupture half-duration reduce tsunami false alarms at fixed recall?
targets.plain: Promising warning-screen value only: rupture half-duration reduces false alarms at 80 percent and 90 percent fixed recall in leave-one-region-out earthquake tests, but not at 95 percent recall. Not an operational tsunami warning system.

targets.label: Semantic-shadow discovery lakehouse
targets.question: Can one lakehouse generate better cross-domain research questions from bacteria, HAB, and ecology data?
targets.plain: Yes as a discovery engine: the latest refresh scanned bacteria, HAB, and whale targets and produced model-lift and lead-lag correlation tables. No correlation is promoted as a claim until it survives an independent reproduction/null-control run.

targets.label: Foundation-model coastal inputs
targets.question: Which foundation-model source data is ready for coastal-hazard experiments?
targets.plain: Source adapters are now separated by readiness: Sentinel-3 OLCI/SLSTR chlorophyll data is present locally for Granite-Ocean style work, and MERRA-2 surface variables are available through Earth Engine for station-level Prithvi inputs. Full global Prithvi-WxC pressure-level rollouts still need NASA GES DISC authorization.

targets.label: Interlingua — a shared physics language
targets.question: Can different regions' coastal data be re-encoded onto one physics 'language' that transfers without local labels?
targets.plain: Caveated yes: hand-built non-dimensional π-groups remain the cleanest label-free cross-region bacteria language, but the newer audits define hard boundaries. The same representation does not carry annual EU bathing-water classes past calendar+memory, and daily freshwater cyano π features do not beat calendar or persistence.
targets.detail: We re-encoded coastal data onto a label-free physics manifold and raced learned encoders against hand-built π-groups. The π-groups won the US/Ireland/Australia bacteria transfer lane and survived permutation-null checks, but the later boundary tests matter: annual EU class resolution and freshwater cyano daily-met transfer do not promote. Interlingua is a useful coordinate system per signal family, not one universal vector across hazards.

deployable_rule.rule: issue a beach advisory when calibrated P(enterococcus exceedance) >= 0.0635
deployable_rule.chosen_on: 2020-21 validation (recall>=0.80), via operating_point_analysis.py — NOT re-tuned on test
deployable_rule.reading: catches 86% of true exceedances; issues 3.35 false advisories per caught exceedance (precision 23%); flags 38% of beach-days.

decision_curve.method: Vickers-Elkin net benefit; valid because the model is calibrated on the retrospective out-of-time proxy (ECE<0.05); prospective lockbox pending
decision_curve.interpretation: across this threshold-probability range the calibrated model beats both 'advise everywhere' and 'advise nowhere'; p_t is the policy's implied cost (one caught exceedance worth (1-p_t)/p_t false advisories).

reports/bacteria/decision_rule.json.honest_cost_note: No fabricated dollar/illness counts. The trade-off is stated in operational units (exceedances caught vs false advisories) + the DCA-implied cost ratio. A site that wants fewer closures raises tau (toward the max-precision point); a site prioritising public health lowers it (toward max-detection). EPA recreational-water practice errs toward warning, i.e. a LOW tau, which this rule reflects.

reports/estate_search/FINDINGS.md.text:
# Estate-wide GPU Signal Search — FINDINGS

**Date:** 2026-06-19/20 · **Compute:** RTX 5090 (torch 2.11.0+cu128, CUDA) · **Orchestration:** `estate-gpu-signal-search` workflow (8 agents: recon + 3×build + 3×adversarial-critic + synthesis)

## Mandate
Jon: "run every possible permutation of all signals" / "use the GPU harder" / "all value no bloat" / "we want all the data even if it doesn't find anything, as long as it's real and accurate." Reframe: we had only ever tested **linear subsets of ~20 hand-picked signals**. This searched the **nonlinear functional-form + interaction space over ~1,000+ pooled estate columns**, with valid multiple-testing control, on the GPU.

## Bottom line
**Clean, validly-searched DRIVER-NULL across the entire functional-form + interaction space.** Zero genuinely new signals survive FDR **and** beat the frozen bacteria baseline (calibrated AP **0.5099**, EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO). Everything high-scoring is rainfall, station-memory, geography/IDs, or self-referential artifacts. The null — at this scale — **is the deliverable.** Nothing ships; headline unchanged.

## Engines (all RECORD = honest null)

### 1. GPU exhaustive interaction scan (epistasis-style) — `interaction_scan_gpu.py`
- **474,825** pairwise interactions over 975 train-usable estate cols, **4.3M pairs/s**, partial-corr-of-(z_i·z_j)-with-additive-residual statistic.
- Control: BH-FDR (q=0.10) → 828 pass; **300-perm max-stat FWER (p<0.05) → 2 pass**; both gated through a per-row HGBT A/B (interaction-over-main-effects + leave-one-beach-out).
- **0 real survivors.** The one temporally-promising pair (Tokyo Bay surface-salinity × its own lag1, ΔAP +0.0117) is per-station memorization → **LOBO −0.0002**, and is two lags of the same Tokyo variable geographically irrelevant to CA beaches.
- Critic: REAL, leakage-controlled, reproduced; synthetic test recovered a planted interaction (stat 0.957) and rejected a pure main effect (~0.04). **Verdict: RECORD.**

### 2. GPU symbolic regression (auto-discover π-groups) — `symbolic_reg_run.py`
- **3,000,000** expression trees (depth≤3, 12-op set) @ ~7k formulas/s, scored vs the additive FEATS+rain **residual**.
- Best in-sample skill 0.0275 **beats** the permutation null (max 0.0121, p=0.032) → real in-sample structure. But **4 of 5 Pareto formulas just rediscover rainfall** (`nasa_power::PRECTOTCORR`).
- The lone OOT/BH-FDR "survivor" (`ratio(exp(roms::v), sqrt(cdip::airTemp))`): **WASH at signal_lab** (ΔAP +0.0014 < 0.005) **and statistically invalid** — its inputs have 0–1 test-era days, so the feature is a near-constant train-mean fill.
- Critic: REAL GPU run, correct bottom line. **Must-fix (do not cite the "survivor"):** ran over a shared cache matrix with residual FIB leakage; enforce per-column train≥15 ∧ test≥8 day coverage + effective-N guard before Fisher-z; permutation-null framing is anti-conservative not conservative. **Verdict: RECORD.**

### 3. Deep knockoffs (DeepLINK/DeepPINK) — `deep_knockoffs_select.py`
- Competitive-input DNN over original+knockoff features, **knockoff+ FDR** (Candès 2018) q=0.10, 5 seeds, null-symmetric W.
- **0 of 321 candidates** (24 frozen FEATS + 297 estate) survive — τ=∞ all seeds, still 0 at q=0.50.
- **Positive control passes:** `station_prior_rate` ranks #1 by importance (correct rediscovery of the known PRIMARY) — but even it can't clear the finite-sample bound → the null is honest but **partly uninformative/underpowered** at our n/p. Reproduces the lab-memory linear model-X result.
- Critic: REAL, valid FDR (not raw top-k), reproduced. **Must-fix:** wide matrix leaked FIB target-family (`ca_safe_to_swim_fib::geomean_*`) + geo/id/depth columns into the candidate set; null unaffected (a leak only manufactures false positives) but the "leakage-free" wording is overstated; report the statistically-preferred `daily` mode. **Verdict: RECORD.**

## Leakage caveat (does NOT change the nulls)
Two of three wide matrices had incomplete exclude filters — FIB target-family columns (`ca_safe_to_swim_fib::result/geomean_30day/geomean_6week`, `ceden_water_chem::Enterococcus`, `wqp::Enterococcus`, `echo_dmr::Enterococci`) and geo/id/depth/row_number columns reached the candidate set. **A leak can only manufacture a false positive; none occurred, so every null stands.** Must be fixed before any future *positive* from these matrices is trusted.

## What survived nothing tells us
The consistent verdict across interaction-space, formula-space, and knockoff-space: **the lever is not a richer functional form over the existing estate.** It is:
1. **A low-autocorrelation target** — bacteria dry-onset slice, `hypoxia_lead`, cyano onset/anomaly — where the driver-null is known to be escapable (vs the near-ceiling pooled daily bacteria series).
2. **A new modality / objective** — molecular SD ddPCR labels, credentialed FES2014/ERA5/CMEMS physics, or an objective change (triage / transfer / lead-time).

## Next actions
1. **DO NOT promote anything.** Headline stays 0.5099. All three = RECORD.
2. **Catalog:** log the three searched-and-null spaces under `bacteria_exceedance` (done — `estate_interaction_2way`, `estate_symbolic_formula`, `estate_deep_knockoffs`).
3. **Leakage correctness-fix** (before any future positive from these matrices): add `ca_safe_to_swim_fib` + `ca_safe_to_swim_fib_excluded` to the gauntlet `EXCLUDE_PATH_RE`, add `result|geomean|Enterococc|coliform` to `EXCLUDE_COL_RE`, and drop geo/id/depth/pressure/row_number columns.
4. **Scale the valid search** to low-autocorrelation targets — the engines are clean and reusable; point them at the dry-onset / hypoxia / cyano-onset targets.
5. **Data, not form:** prioritize the blocked credentialed fetches (FES2014/ERA5/CMEMS) and molecular labels over more in-estate search.

## Reproduce
```
# interaction scan
python research/estate_search/interaction_scan_matrix.py --max-rows-per-table 120000
python research/estate_search/interaction_scan_gpu.py --target bacteria_exceedance --n-perm 300 --top-ab 8
# symbolic regression
python -m research.estate_search.symbolic_reg_matrix --rebuild
python -m research.estate_search.symbolic_reg_run --n-formulas 3000000 --batch 8000 --n-perm 30 --seed 42
# deep knockoffs
python research/estate_search/deep_knockoffs_wide_matrix.py
python research/estate_search/deep_knockoffs_select.py --q 0.1 --seeds 5 --epochs 150 --mode daily
```

symbolic.engine: symbolic_reg_run (GPU pi-group search) RETARGETED to bacteria onset slice
symbolic.verdict: NULL after FDR: no Pareto formula's out-of-time onset residual corr survives BH-FDR.

reports/findings_campaign/bacteria_dry_o.verdict: DRY_ONSET_NULL: NULL after FDR: no Pareto formula's out-of-time onset residual corr survives BH-FDR.

reports/experiment_program/H1/claims_ind.artifact: Monterey Bay AI Lab — Trust-Layer Claims Index (H1)
reports/experiment_program/H1/claims_ind.purpose: External-facing, auditable index of every promoted claim and every logged null, joined from the signal ledger, the experiment-program gate ledger, and the frozen headline. Read-only join; invents no metric; each row carries its evidence path.

verdict_vocabulary.PRIMARY: This signal IS (the core of) the validated model for that target.
verdict_vocabulary.KEEP: Passed the promotion gate as a feature (temporal dAP >= 0.005 AND survives leave-one-beach-out).
verdict_vocabulary.WASH: Tested as a feature; no transferable lift (|dAP| < 0.005 or sign-flips across strata). A logged null.
verdict_vocabulary.REJECT: Tested; regressed the target (dAP < -0.005). A logged negative.
verdict_vocabulary.UNTESTED: Plausible but NOT yet gated for that target. No claim is made.
verdict_vocabulary.CONFIRMED: (interactions) A real interaction effect, but below the operational lift bar.
verdict_vocabulary.REDUNDANT: Subsumed by a signal already in the model.

availability_disclosure.note: The frozen AP is scored with a 2-day fixed reveal-lag proxy, not the true per-sample available_at timestamp (measured p90 ~11-15d). Under true availability the AP is conservatively ~0.02 lower. This field discloses the delta so the headline is not read as available_at-clean.

lockbox.rule: Samples with sample_date STRICTLY AFTER freeze_cutoff are the prospective lockbox. They must never be used for training, calibration, or signal gating. The headline is scored on them, untouched, by research/bacteria/prospective_scorecard.py.

target_headlines.question: Will a beach's water exceed the AB411 enterococcus standard (>104 MPN/100mL)?
target_headlines.headline: EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO calibrated AP 0.5099 / ROC-AUC 0.868 / ECE 0.009 (incl. harmonic spring-neap tidal escape +0.011 AP over rain+discharge; re-frozen 2026-06-18 from 0.4992); deploy-ready 8/9 counties. OUT-OF-TIME STABLE 2022-2026 (frozen train<=2019; ROC-AUC 0.84-0.88, ECE<0.05 every year; AP swings track base rate not skill) — reports/bacteria/prospective_backtest.json. True forward lockbox (freeze_cutoff 2026-06-18) accrues post-cutoff samples.

target_headlines.question: Will a pier's next sample exceed particulate domoic acid 500 ng/L (=0.5 ng/mL)?
target_headlines.headline: SUGGESTIVE, NOT ESTABLISHED (eval_standard ClaimCard 2026-06-19, reports/_eval_cards/da_card.json): AP 0.203 (drifted down from 0.232) / ROC-AUC 0.842 time-holdout; the STRONGEST baseline is PERSISTENCE (AP 0.138), not seasonal-naive, margin +0.065; perm-null p=0.005 (ranking is real) and jackknife-stable, BUT the station-clustered bootstrap CI crosses zero at the median (only 27% of seeds clear 0, 0% clear +0.005) and LOSO is 5/8 -> directionally real yet underpowered. 'beats seasonal-naive 8/8' is the easy comparison. The 'beats C-HARM 8/8 ~10x' framing is a BROKEN-BASELINE MIRAGE (C-HARM sub-random, ROC-AUC 0.30); see reports/hab/charm_comparison.json.

target_headlines.question: Does a label-free representation rank enterococcus exceedance ZERO-SHOT in a country with no local training labels?
target_headlines.headline: zero-shot US<->IE mean AP 0.136; CORRECTED mean lift 1.91× vs matched-prevalence permutation null (NOT 2.25× vs base; inflation 1.18×); US->IE durable (p 0.020), IE->US marginal (p 0.039); beats the learned encoders + model zoo
target_headlines.baseline_to_beat: target-country base rate + within-stratum permutation null; raw-π is the champion to beat
target_headlines.coverage: CA + Ireland marine sites, weather-only physics, 1990-2026

target_headlines.question: Will bottom water go hypoxic (DO_min <= 2 mg/L) 7-14 days ahead?
target_headlines.headline: the driver-null's first clean ESCAPE — STRAT lift +0.021 AP @7-14d over persist+season. HARDENED 2026-06-19 (eval_standard ClaimCard, reports/_eval_cards/hypoxia_card.json): survives a permutation null (p=0.005) AND BH-FDR across all 5 horizons (h7 q=0.0083, h14 q=0.0083 SURVIVE; 21d +0.006 and 28d +0.003 correctly WASH) AND a station-clustered bootstrap CI [0.011, 0.053]; decision-curve valuable across the whole cost grid; strongest in the currently-oxic-origin onset slice (+0.029 = genuine lead-skill). Scope is 7-14d (not 7d+). Carrier = thermal cap + warmth, NOT haline/wind/chl; deep nets (Transformer/GRU) do not beat the tree
target_headlines.baseline_to_beat: raw persistence + seasonal climatology + persist+season HGBT (station-block bootstrap CI on the lift)
target_headlines.coverage: Tokyo Bay TBEIC, 7 stations, 2009-2026; ~6,166 hypoxia days (28-30% base rate); 4 of 7 stations have test-era events, so the station-block CI is effectively driven by 4 (lift still +ve in all 4)

target_headlines.question: Among currently-NON-hypoxic deep-basin origins (bottom DO_min > 2 mg/L), will hypoxia BEGIN within (T, T+h]? (G2 aquaculture-wedge reframe of hypoxia_lead.)
target_headlines.headline: ONSET reframe WEAKENS the hypoxia_lead escape: STRAT lift is CI-separated ONLY at h=3d (+0.0545 AP, station-block CI [0.014,0.084]) and washes (CI includes 0) at every multi-week lead 7-28d in the TEMPORAL holdout. The two holdouts DISAGREE — leave-station-out shows ~0 lift at h=3 but +0.028 at h=7 — so NO single horizon survives BOTH holdouts CI-separated. Verdict: WASH at deployable multi-week lead. The model still beats the better naive at every h (seasonality + DO-proximity), but the *stratification driver* adds no robust onset lead-skill beyond persist+season. The parent state-target '+0.02 @7-14d' does not transfer to the harder onset decision.
target_headlines.baseline_to_beat: seasonal-naive (month onset-rate climatology) + DO-proximity null + persist+season HGBT; station-block bootstrap CI on the STRAT lift, plus leave-station-out as a 2nd holdout
target_headlines.coverage: Tokyo Bay TBEIC, 7 stations, 2009-2026; onset base rate 0.14 (h=3) -> 0.36 (h=28); 601-1568 test-era onset events per horizon (none small-N); single basin

target_headlines.question: Do prior-period ocean drivers predict CA cetacean mortality (iNat proxy) beyond seasonal climatology?
target_headlines.headline: prey-collapse pathway corroborated by TWO forage indices (krill RR 0.83, rockfish RR 0.79, both cluster-significant) but SUGGESTIVE not robust (fail the 17-year-cluster bootstrap); acute-DA panel link NOT ROBUST; the DA->death link rests on case-level necropsy, not panel inference
target_headlines.best_model: effort-offset Poisson GLM (research/whale/mortality_model.py); NO panel link survives small-cluster-robust inference
target_headlines.baseline_to_beat: effort-aware per-(region,taxon,calendar-month) seasonal climatology; pairs year-block bootstrap CI must exclude 1.0
target_headlines.coverage: CA 3 coast regions, monthly 2010-2026; 675 death-months, ~17 year-clusters (iNaturalist citizen-science proxy)

target_headlines.question: Among days a fjord deep-basin is anoxic, will an episodic dense-water RENEWAL re-oxygenate it (DO_min > 2 mg/L) within ~3 weeks?
target_headlines.headline: the slow-driver principle PARTIALLY transfers to a 2nd basin once carrier+objective match the physics: deep-water density gives +0.08 AP renewal-onset lead-skill that is YEAR-ROBUST only at ~21-day lead (positive in all 4 test years); shorter 7-14d leads are fragile (carried by 2024-25, hurt in 2019-20). DENSITY >> THERMAL. Single-inlet, discovery-grade
target_headlines.best_model: season + recent-DO context + deep-water density HGBT (research/predict10x/saanich_renewal_onset.py)
target_headlines.baseline_to_beat: season + recent-DO trajectory (30-day moving-block bootstrap CI + per-test-year sign-consistency)
target_headlines.coverage: Saanich Inlet ONC deep basin (73-94 m); train 2012-2017 (SPARSE, 2014/15 absent), test 2018-2025 with a 2022-23 gap, renewal flips concentrated in 2024-25; single location, ~20 episodes / ~4 effective years

target_headlines.question: Will SF Bay see an extreme still-water-level day (daily-max total water level >= 99th pct, 2.248 m MLLW) -- the coastal-flood trigger; and is it COMPOUND (co-occurring with rain/river wetness)?
target_headlines.headline: DRIVER-LIFT CEILING (WASH): the free harmonic TIDE TABLE alone is the best naive (AP 0.223 / AUROC 0.945); full driver model AP 0.294 / AUROC 0.965 but the +0.069 AP over tide-only is NOT CI-separated (paired-bootstrap [-0.070,+0.206], N=62 OOF events). Perm-null clean (p 0.005) but signal is mostly the deterministic tide (drop-tide AP 0.201). ROBUST positive is STRUCTURAL: ~50% of extreme-water-level days are COMPOUND (surge+rain/river co-occur) -> correlated-risk wedge for insurer reserving, not driver-lift. Driver-null law holds for a new hazard/basin.
target_headlines.best_model: isotonic HGBT on harmonic-tide + surge + rain + river + memory (reports/experiment_program/G1/model_g1.py)
target_headlines.baseline_to_beat: BETTER of persistence, month-seasonal-naive, and the harmonic tide table (the tide table dominates)
target_headlines.coverage: NOAA CO-OPS 9414290 (SF Embarcadero) + gridMET (37.5,-122.5) + USGS SF-Bay-watershed discharge; daily 2005-2026, 7801 days, 79 high-water / 40 compound days (small-N)

Zero-shot Chronos-Bolt floor: bacteria AP 0.827, beats persistence +0.080 (perm-null p=0.005, 13/16 counties). Best learner; beats A2/A3.
NO CROSS-HAZARD TRANSFER: dAP +0.018 bacteria (below +0.03 gate), -0.007 DA (noise). 'Unified' = deployment convenience, NOT scientific skill.

All-data ablation: skill NOT monotone; saturates after first memory family, all-data endpoint degrades below early peak. Both clear stronger-naive from rung 1.

FORWARD TEST PASS: frozen model AP 0.6335/AUROC 0.848 on unseen 2026 holdout (n=2487, base 0.186); beats station-memory 0.368 by +0.265. CAVEAT: temporal holdout, not yet prospective (pristine lockbox empty); AP not comparable to frozen (base-rate confound), AUROC held.

DRIVER-NULL ESCAPE REPLICATED: Russian R. upstream turbidity (Hopland) -> Guerneville intake, lead-2 dAP +0.049 (block-CI [0.035,0.066], perm-null p<0.005). 2nd advection escape after Klamath. Water-utility product.
Regime-conditional drivers FAIL strict block-CI: storm regime grazes bar (+0.0254, block-CI [-0.0005,0.0517] incl 0); heatwave NEGATIVE. DA blocked-thin. Near-miss, not separated.

TAIL-ONLY driver skill PASS: wet/storm tail driver-aug AP +0.060 (CI [0.048,0.072], perm-null p<0.005), recall@p70 +0.14. Driver-null HOLDS on mean/dry-onset/memory-tail/DA. Drivers help only on wet extremes.

Interlingua transfer SUPRA-SEASONAL both dirs but STATION-MEMORY-BOUND: US->IE ties site-memory (+0.030, CI incl 0); IE->US beats by thin +0.022 (CI excl 0). Critique reproduces (pi_minus_uv > all5). Re-rate KEEP-weak.
Expanded pi-library NULL: no new group (Richardson/Stokes/residence-time) beats UV-only bar 0.1446. Confirms pi-groups redundant/saturated.

Upstream-lead sweep: screened ~2386 pairs, found 1 NEW advective escape of 23 (Cache Slough 11455350->11455335, dAP +0.108 lead-1, CI [0.059,0.140], perm-null p<0.005); B1 control reproduced. Driver-null HOLDS for 22/23 -> advection escapes are RARE/special-case, not general. New escape caveated (possible tidal-phase artifact).

deployable (exclude-SD): temporal ΔAP +0.0106 net vs FEATS+rain, LOBO 59% beaches positive (heavy tail, p10 -0.048/p90 +0.080), perm-null p<0.005. CRITIC-CONFIRMED SURVIVES (2026-06-16); ~half the raw tide lift is a wet-season/rain proxy, +0.0106 is the rain-orthogonal remainder. HARMONIC CONFIRMATION (2026-06-18, vs FEATS+rain+discharge): the deterministic tide-table range (utide, 68 constituents/gauge) alone gives temporal ΔAP +0.0118 (>observed range +0.0095) and leave-one-GAUGE-out +0.0135 (3/3 gauges) — so the escape transfers to UNSEEN gauges and is the astronomical envelope, not surge. LEAKAGE-FREE: train-only (≤2019) refit reproduces 2022+ range at corr 0.99993 and holds ΔAP +0.0124 / LOGO +0.0151. The lunar_phase REJECT (−0.006) was a too-crude synodic-cosine proxy, not absence of mechanism. COVERAGE CORRECTION 2026-06-18 (critic): harmonic range is utide-solved at 3-5 GAUGES and broadcast (≤390 km), NOT computed per-beach — utide needs an observed level series; per-beach needs FES2014/OTPS (blocked). The 5-gauge thinness is NOT dissolved. The +0.009/+0.0118 has no bootstrap CI yet and the perm-null p<0.005 was the observed-range run, not the harmonic feature. SPATIAL-GENERALIZATION OVERTURNED 2026-06-18 (boost-sweep, independently verified): expanding folds 3→14 (27 NOAA harcon stations, median beach→station 76km→11.5km) COLLAPSES leave-one-REGION-out to ΔAP +0.0021 (8/14 positive, per-region −0.024..+0.037) and softens the harmonic perm-null to p=0.09 — neither clears the bar. The strong '3/3 gauges, LOGO +0.0135, perm 0.01' was a NON-REPRESENTATIVE 3-gauge subset artifact. The POOLED temporal lift (+0.009) and the frozen-headline pooled delta (+0.0091 cal AP) still hold and reproduce, but the spring-neap signal does NOT robustly generalize across regions. Honest status: real pooled lift, weak/unproven spatial transfer.

shadow_report.excluded_rule: Use only lag/rolling all-raw shadow columns and l2_openmeteo lag/rolling columns; exclude labels, risks, whale, imputed DA, hazards, and production outputs.
