reports/hab/interlingua_cyano_transfer_d.hypothesis: REFUTATION of the monthly-met NULL: does the physics-pi interlingua manifold transfer to freshwater cyano when pi is built from DAILY (not monthly) met? Monthly smoothing handicapped pi's warm-spell/stratification/antecedent-precip channels; the pre-registered fix is daily resolution.

data.met_source: NASA POWER daily point (keyless; ERA5/Open-Meteo quota-blocked mid-fetch)
data.met_resolution: DAILY met, antecedent windows ending at each weekly obs: long=28d, short=7d (zero future leakage)

reports/hab/interlingua_cyano_transfer_d.held_out_scheme: leave-one-REGION(state)-out; held-out region's lakes NEVER in training; scorable region needs >= 300 rows & 30 events
reports/hab/interlingua_cyano_transfer_d.estimator: standardized class-balanced L2 logistic ONLY (linear; HGBT inflates at this S/N)
reports/hab/interlingua_cyano_transfer_d.verdict: NULL-CONFIRMED (no escape) — daily-met pi-minus-calendar CI95 STRADDLES 0 [-0.0298, 0.0147] with mean pi 0.4155 <= calendar 0.4242; physics does not separate from seasonality on held-out lakes. Daily resolution did NOT flip the monthly null. Daily NARROWED the pi-vs-calendar gap from -0.0338 (monthly) to -0.0087, but did not close it.
reports/hab/interlingua_cyano_transfer_d.decision_rule: pi-minus-calendar CI95 strictly >0 on held-out lakes => SIGNAL-EMERGES (flag for critic). CI95 <=0 / overlapping => NULL-CONFIRMED-STRONGER. <5 regions => UNDERPOWERED.

reports/hab/cyano/mcye_alberta.json.dataset: Alberta open-data cyanobacterial bloom monitoring (OGL-Alberta, keyless)

robustness.detection_conditional_note: headline is among DETECTABLE samples (the bloom regime). On the full population incl. non-detects the rank stat dilutes -- expected zero-inflation (no bloom -> no toxin -> no toxic genes), not a refutation.

reports/hab/cyano/mcye_alberta.json.verdict: DEFINITIVE REPLICATION at scale: toxic-genotype (mcyE) -> microcystin beyond biomass holds across 55 lakes and 5 years (rho 0.6824, perm-p 0.0), every lake and every year positive, in the detectable/bloom regime. The single-season Erie finding is now a large-n, multi-lake, multi-year established effect.

reports/bacteria/breakthrough_gate/break.claim: Early-warning marine enterococcus exceedance forecasting on currently-clean beaches.
reports/bacteria/breakthrough_gate/break.final_breakthrough_reason: FROZEN_HEADLINE.json has scored_forward=false; score post-freeze rows before claiming final breakthrough.

lockbox.rule: Samples with sample_date STRICTLY AFTER freeze_cutoff are the prospective lockbox. They must never be used for training, calibration, or signal gating. The headline is scored on them, untouched, by research/bacteria/prospective_scorecard.py.

checks.threshold: frozen=true and model AP equals FROZEN_HEADLINE headline AP
checks.why: Guards against pooled or stale headline artifacts.
The deployable stratum must clear an absolute precision-recall bar.
The model must beat the strongest operational comparator, not just a weak baseline.
A deployable warning claim must remain calibrated.
The onset slice must be tied to the same sealed headline run.
The core science claim is clean-to-dirty early warning over station memory.
The onset lift should be large enough to survive ordinary metric noise.
The result must travel across counties against the rain-rule incumbent.
The result must travel across counties against station memory.
Calibration must be deployable in most county holdouts.

no_bloat_policy: Use only sealed, hashable inputs for the headline: beach observations, rainfall, discharge, harmonic tide, and station geometry.
Keep the headline fixed to label=enterococcus, reveal_lag_days=2, EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO, train<=2019, calibrate=2020-2021, test>=2022.
Treat OOI, satellite, HRRR, ERA5, new hydrology, and discovery inventories as add-on candidate tracks until separately pre-registered.
Do not use live fetches, foundation-model bakeoffs, San Diego pooled gains, or weak-baseline comparisons to justify the breakthrough claim.
The final claim requires post-freeze lockbox scoring with no retraining, threshold tuning, calibration updates, or signal gating.
reports/bacteria/breakthrough_gate/break.blocking_next_step: Run research/bacteria/prospective_scorecard.py on samples strictly after the freeze_cutoff, with no retraining, threshold tuning, calibration updates, or signal gating.

bacteria_results/international/unified_z.question: Leave-one-basin-out zero-shot FIB ranking with interlingua pi, model zoo, and optional CARTE.
bacteria_results/international/unified_z.anti_leak_rule: Inputs are exogenous weather, pi groups, calendar, optional geography, and optional strings only. No FIB concentration, hazard, exceedance, lab-history, or target-domain labels are used as model inputs.

physics_kernel_screen.survivor_rule: mean AP delta >= +0.005, paired-domain delta CI lower > 0, <=2 target regressions worse than -0.002 AP, and >=10 domains with >=80% complete source rows

domain_bootstrap.unit: held-out domain paired with frozen pi+logistic

survival_gate.rule: Freeze only if every target beats base rate, at least 80% beat the source-stratum permutation-null p95, each target has >=100 null permutations, and the held-out-domain bootstrap lower bound is above zero for AP-base and above one for lift. If the primary arm is not the frozen pi baseline, it must also beat frozen pi+logistic by >=0.005 mean AP, with paired-domain delta CI lower bound above zero and at most two >0.002 AP regressions.

carte_zero_shot.note: CARTE rows are run on a capped fair sample because CARTE is a small-n tabular-FM method and is too slow for the full 400k-row panel.

bacteria_results/international/unified_z.verdict: Best mean-AP numeric zero-shot arm is pi + logistic (mean AP 0.1511, mean lift 1.752, min lift 1.186 over 12 targets). The configured pi+logistic arm beats its within-source-stratum permutation null on 12/12 tested targets. CARTE completed 0 sampled target-arms; compare those only against same-sample numeric baselines.

reports/hab/cyano/cyano_onset_claim_card.question: Does the continental cyano-onset law survive adversarial claim-card checks?
reports/hab/cyano/cyano_onset_claim_card.headline: Latitude/quadratic geography is a real cold-start cyano-onset law under held-out state, lat-band, and year tests; do not sell the weak photoperiod-only mechanism, and treat winter met as a secondary hardening path.

photoperiod_note.read: photoperiod-only is interpretive/weak; it is not the claim anchor.

current_year_censor_check_exclude_2026.read: Claim cannot depend on partial/current 2026 if this stays positive.

forecast_valid_prior_winter_met_competit.read: If winter met materially beats lat-only on held-out regions, the publishable claim is a geography+forecast-weather law, not pure latitude.

claim_card.claim: Cold-start first high/very-high CyAN bloom onset DOY is partly predictable from latitude in held-out US regions.
do_not_claim: the law predicts bloom abundance or persistence
the law is validated outside CyAN lakes or outside the US panel
claim_card.decision_value: Useful for when to start cyano sampling/remote-sensing attention at lakes with no local history.
claim_card.next_redteam: Hold out a post-2026 year and a non-US freshwater panel if one is staged.

reports/predict10x/hypoxia_gru.json.what: GENUINE GRU temporal net vs HGBT-strat + persistence for multi-week hypoxia lead
reports/predict10x/hypoxia_gru.json.read: If GRU beats HGBT-strat with CI>0 at multi-week lead, the genuine temporal model extracts dynamics the tree misses. If gru-hgbt ~0, the +0.02 ceiling is real.

interaction.engine: interaction_scan_gpu (epistasis 2-way) RETARGETED to bacteria onset slice

panel.baseline: DEPLOYED FEATS+rain HGBT (frozen), residual on onset slice

fdr.method: BH-FDR q=0.10 on Fisher-z partial-corr p AND perm max-null FWER p<0.05

interaction.verdict: 1 interaction(s) add onset-slice AP over the deployed model — INSPECT for station-memory/rain/autocorrelation artifacts before any ship.

reports/findings_campaign/bacteria_dry_o.verdict: DRY_ONSET_NULL: 1 interaction(s) add onset-slice AP over the deployed model — INSPECT for station-memory/rain/autocorrelation artifacts before any ship.

reports/bacteria/carte_critic.json._verdict: FAIL (overclaimed). CARTE's win is a BASELINE-WEAKNESS artifact, not a shared-plane/identity win. (a) numeric-only CARTE (strings dropped) AUC 0.759 >= CARTE-full 0.755 -> edge is NOT from station-identity strings, refuting the station-memorization confound. (b) But fair NUMERIC baselines on the same 15 metrics beat CARTE: RandomForest 0.774-0.780 (beats CARTE every seed), logistic 0.742-0.764 (ties), regularized HGBT 0.737-0.756. The pilot's 'raw' = unregularized HGBT(max_iter=400) overfits at n=2000 -> a strawman bar. CARTE clears only the strawman, loses to a vanilla RF on identical features. (c) station overlap test<->train pool = 97.6%; target-encoded identity does NOT close to CARTE (fair-raw CI [-0.021,0.013] incl 0) because at n=2000 per-station counts are too sparse to encode.

reports/lead_time/karenia_watchlist_2026.carrier: site-recurrence x target-week season (keyless); seasonal CALENDAR not a forecast
reports/lead_time/karenia_watchlist_2026.honest_claim: ~+0.20-0.23 more onset events caught vs site-history ranking at 20% budget, 1-40wk out, Florida-scoped, seasonal-recurrence (not weather forecast)

reports/experiment_program/B1/b1_results.title: Russian River advection replication (driver-null escape test)
reports/experiment_program/B1/b1_results.headline: ESCAPE REPLICATED on Russian River: upstream turbidity (Hopland 11462500) as a lead feature adds dAP=0.049 (CI 0.035..0.066) over the downstream-persistence baseline at the 2-day horizon -- CI fully above +0.03. Upstream-ALONE does not beat persistence (it is marginal-over-persistence). At lead=1d the lift is only +0.025 (persistence acf=0.939 too strong); the escape opens once downstream memory decays. Klamath upstream->downstream advection win generalizes to a Sonoma Water intake basin.

permutation_null.method: block-shuffle upstream features by water-year, 200 iters, lead=2d, LOBO OOF
permutation_null.interpretation: advective lift collapses under block shuffle -> not an autocorrelation artifact

erie_reference.note: single-season Erie 2022 values this national test tries to replicate

C_fraction_with_biomass_IDEAL.reason: public WQP programs do not co-measure mcyE + total-cyano (for the fraction) + chlorophyll (biomass) + microcystin at >=15 site-dates; the genus-specific fraction program carries NO chlorophyll even within +-10 d. The stronger Erie fraction signal (+0.65) stays single-system; needs a program co-measuring all four.

geographic_generalization.note: non-Ohio = New England/Mid-Atlantic/Upper-Midwest/Texas; tests whether the effect is regional (Ohio, ~Erie's region) or genuinely geographically general.

verdict.headline: REPLICATES: mcyE genotype -> microcystin beyond biomass holds across independent lakes AND years (national WQP). The Erie finding generalizes.
verdict.honest_note: Fraction-with-biomass (Erie's +0.65) is NOT cleanly testable nationally: the WQP program carrying total-cyano lacks chlorophyll. mcyE-absolute (Erie +0.21) IS testable with biomass control -- that is test A.

reports/hab/erie/ERIE_CYANO_DRIVER_BREAK.text:
# Western Lake Erie microcystin — does nutrient LOADING break the cyano driver-null?

**Two-Claude campaign, 2026-06-19. Verdict: NO. The cyano driver-null HOLDS — now tested
with the two levers the satellite work could never reach: real TOXIN labels and gold-standard
tributary nutrient LOADING flux.**

## Why this was the genuinely-open question
The national CyAN-satellite cyano work ([[algae-bloom-frontier]]) found bloom *occurrence* is
governed by satellite-CI autocorrelation + seasonal climatology, with no dynamic weather or
nutrient driver lifting skill once season + lake-climate were controlled. But CyAN cannot see
**microcystin toxin**, and no national feed carries **tributary nutrient loading**. The memory
named exactly these as the only untested levers for a real cyano breakthrough. Western Lake
Erie is the one system where both exist on disk:

- **GLERL/NOAA western-basin field sampling** (`glerl_lake_erie_hab_wq`): in-situ
  **particulate microcystin** + chlorophyll + phycocyanin + lake TP/SRP/N at the WE2–WE16
  transect, 2012–2019 (641 toxin obs; the 2020–2022 file layout drops station id).
- **Heidelberg Tributary Loading Program** (`heidelberg_htlp`): **Maumee River** near-daily
  flow + TP + SRP, ~500 samples/yr — the exact data NOAA's seasonal bloom-severity forecast
  is built on.

Western Lake Erie *Microcystis* is the textbook nutrient-driven HAB. If the driver-null was
going to break anywhere, it was here.

## What we tested (3 independent lanes)

**Lane A — loading FORECAST gate** (`research/hab/erie_microcystin_driver_gate.py`).
Target = next-visit particulate microcystin > 1.6 µg/L (Ohio/WHO advisory) at a WE station,
chained within-season (gap ≤ 24 d). Baseline = rich history + season + station (lagged
microcystin/chl/phyco, day-of-year, station). Driver = 22 antecedent **Maumee loading**
features (integrated TP & SRP load, flow, flow-weighted conc over 7/14/28/56 d + spring
cumulative). Eval = leave-one-YEAR-out AP (blooms cluster by year). Gates = ΔAP ≥ 0.005 +
perm-null + noise-column floor.

> Result: **ΔAP = −0.028** (loading *hurts*), perm-p **0.92**, noise-floor q95 **+0.033**,
> baseline AP 0.629 → +loading 0.601. 554 pairs, 10 stations, 8 years. **NULL.**

**Lane B — in-situ nutrient + NOAA replication** (`research/hab/erie_insitu_nutrient_gate.py`,
claude-f). Contemporaneous lake nutrients vs microcystin, de-seasoned + within-station:
only **lake TP** keeps a partial association (rho 0.225, perm-p 2e-4) — and that is bloom
**co-product** co-variation (cells contain P), not a forecast driver. SRP, N, TP:N all wash.
Year-level NOAA spring-load→severity (n=8): directionally consistent for **chlorophyll**
(rho 0.74, p=0.04) but **not** for the toxin (microcystin-median rho 0.52, p=0.19).

**Critic — adversarial refutation** (claude-critic, independent from-raw reimplementation).
Attacked the null 5 ways: weaker/seasononly baselines (loading lift negative at every level);
WHO >8 µg/L target (−0.053); chlorophyll target (−0.030, perm-p 0.96); phycocyanin (+0.011,
perm-p 0.12 fails); the canonical NOAA spring→Aug–Sep framing (+0.015 AP but **year-perm-p
0.156**, collapses at n=8); load math + power. **NULL SURVIVES every attack.**

**Lane C — buoyancy mechanism (calm + warm)** (`research/hab/erie_strat_met_gate.py`).
The remaining in-lake lever: *Microcystis* floats into surface scum in sustained calm, warm
water. GLERL casts are surface-only (no true stratification), so we tested the proxy —
in-situ physical state (lagged) + antecedent NASA POWER met (calm-fraction, longest calm run,
warmth+anomaly, solar, precip over 3/7/14 d at the WE centroid). The automated gate initially
flagged a "pass," but **decomposition killed it as a false positive** (`erie_strat_met_decomp.json`):
the actual calm/warm **met washes** (ΔAP −0.017, perm-p 0.75); the lone passing arm (6 in-situ
features, +0.032) does not localize — optical bloom-proxies (Secchi/turbidity/DO) are null, the
physical group sits *exactly* on its noise floor (+0.023 vs floor +0.023), and **no single feature
clears its own noise floor**. It was small-n multi-feature HGBT noise inflation that luck-passed
1 of 3 arm perm-nulls — the Alexandrium +0.04-noise-floor trap again. **Buoyancy lever: NULL.**

## The honest scientific conclusion
1. **Loading sets the seasonal/annual ENVELOPE, not the sub-seasonal forecast.** Spring P load
   tracks that year's *chlorophyll* (biomass) — consistent with the published Lake Erie
   relationship — but provides **no station-level forecast skill** for microcystin occurrence
   once season + autocorrelation are controlled. Minimum detectable ΔAP at this N is ~0.02–0.03;
   every observed loading effect is ≤ 0.
2. **Toxin ≠ biomass.** Loading relates to biomass (chl rho 0.74) far better than to the toxin
   (mc rho 0.52, n.s.). Microcystin is governed by strain/regulation dynamics loading can't see.
3. **The cyano driver-null now generalizes to the hardest possible test** — real toxin labels +
   gold-standard tributary loading in the most nutrient-driven freshwater HAB on Earth. This
   closes the two levers [[algae-bloom-frontier]] left open. It is a definitive **negative**,
   not a manufactured breakthrough.

## National generalization (the properly-powered test)
Erie is one system (n=554 pairs, 8 years) — and Lane C showed n=554 has a +0.03 AP noise
floor that can fake a "pass." So we built the **first national in-situ microcystin toxin
corpus** to test the driver-null with real power:

- `data/external_raw/wqp_microcystin/` — 68,924 WQP grab-sample microcystin measurements,
  5,905 sites, 2008–2024 (`ops/data_fetch/fetch_wqp_microcystin.py`). Censoring handled
  (non-detects "<0.13"/"ND"/"U" → below-threshold, not dropped — they're 18% of data).
- `data/external_curated/wqp_microcystin_panel/panel.parquet` — 32,812-visit panel, 3,564
  sites, **49 states** (`research/hab/build_national_microcystin_panel.py`).
- `data/external_raw/wqp_microcystin_met/` — per-site NASA POWER daily met, 432 well-sampled
  sites (`ops/data_fetch/fetch_nasapower_microcystin_sites.py`).

**National forecastability** (new result): in-situ **toxin** blooms forecast at pooled LOYO AP
**0.774** (vs persistence ~0.56) from history+season+site alone — the CyAN *satellite* finding
([[algae-bloom-frontier]]) now confirmed on the real toxin, 49 states. NOTE (critic-2 caveat):
the pooled AP is optimistic vs the **macro within-site AP ~0.57** (mean within-site base rate
0.285) — cross-site ranking inflates the pooled number; the honest per-lake forecast skill is
the within-site lift (+0.28, 96.6% of sites beat their own base rate). CENSORING FIX (critic-2):
the panel builder originally dropped ~13.8k non-detects with a blank result-unit; recovering
them via the detection-limit fields lowered the base rate 0.194→0.141 and the headline ~0.80→0.78.

**National driver test** (`research/hab/national_microcystin_driver_gate.py`): adding 18
antecedent met features (calm-fraction, warmth+anomaly, solar, precip, wind over 7/14/30 d)
to that baseline, evaluated under BOTH leave-year-out and grouped leave-site-out:

> Result (corrected censoring-fixed panel): ΔAP **−0.007** (leave-year-out) and **−0.0024**
> (leave-site-out) — both below the +0.005 gate. **Noise floor is NEGATIVE** at n~21k — random
> features cannot manufacture lift here, unlike Erie's n=554. **NULL, robustly.** Met hurts even
> vs a season-only baseline; met-alone AP (0.335) ≈ the base rate. Zero forecast skill nationally.

This is the decisive generalization: the cyano driver-null holds for the real toxin, at
national scale (431 lakes / 49 states), against meteorology — and the negative noise floor
proves the result is power-real, not an artifact. It also explains Erie's Lane C false
positive (small-n inflated floor).

## What WOULD still be worth trying (kept, not done)
- **In-lake stratification / water-column temperature** at the station (vertical structure
  drives *Microcystis* buoyancy) — a different mechanism than upstream loading.
- **Strain / qPCR mcyE toxin-gene** labels — the toxin-regulation lever, genuinely unseen here.
- n is the wall on the seasonal relationship: more bloom-years (recover 2020–2022 station ids;
  add other GLERL/IFCB basins) would let the spring-load→severity relation be properly null-tested.

## Reproduce
```
python research/hab/erie_microcystin_driver_gate.py     # Lane A  -> erie_microcystin_driver_gate.json
python research/hab/erie_insitu_nutrient_gate.py        # Lane B  -> erie_insitu_nutrient_gate.json
# critic probes: reports/hab/erie/_critic_probe.py, _critic_springgate.py
```
Coordination + ledger + learnings: `reports/hab/erie/{COORDINATION,erie_intents.json,erie_learnings.jsonl}`.

Not a clean early-warning win.

reports/lead_time/cyano_watchlist_202605.honest_claim: ~5-7 pts more new blooms caught vs status-quo lake-memory at 5-20wk lead; recurrence+season only; multi-week gain is broadly distributed across states

reports/interlingua_eu_transfer.json.hypothesis: The proven physics-pi interlingua that ranks coastal-FIB marine exceedance zero-shot across CA/IE/FL/AU_NSW/HI extends to a NEW CONTINENT'S coasts (pan-EU bathing water) — testing whether the invariance manifold is GLOBAL.
reports/interlingua_eu_transfer.json.held_out_scheme: leave-COUNTRY-out (Part A: train US donor, zero-shot rank each held-out EU country) + leave-SITE-out resolution control (Part B: proven FL donor, daily vs EEA-style annual class). NEVER random rows.
reports/interlingua_eu_transfer.json.label: Part A: EU Bathing Water Directive annual class binarized (Poor|Sufficient='bad'). Part B/donor: marine enterococcus exceedance ent>104 cfu/100ml (US-EPA marine BAV).
reports/interlingua_eu_transfer.json.baselines: calendar-only (interlingua signature control) AND site-memory (per-site/LOYO historical bad-rate). Gates: permutation-null p + bootstrap 95% CI on lift.
reports/interlingua_eu_transfer.json.data_resolution_caveat: EEA is ANNUAL-CLASS only: one row per (site, year), categorical qualityStatus, and a CONSTANT July-1 PLACEHOLDER date (no per-sample FIB, no sample date). The proven pi transfer is a PER-EVENT DAILY test; with a placeholder date the EU pi is a per-site July CLIMATOLOGY (a geography proxy), NOT a per-event physics signal. Part B quantifies how much skill this coarsening alone destroys on a PROVEN donor, to separate a RESOLUTION boundary from a GEOGRAPHIC one.
reports/interlingua_eu_transfer.json.verdict_reason: the donor's pi SURVIVES annual coarsening (so resolution is not the blocker) yet pi does NOT out-rank calendar+memory on held-out EU coasts — a genuine geographic boundary: the manifold does not generalize to EU bathing water.
reports/interlingua_eu_transfer.json.what_would_refute_it: ESCAPE would be refuted by a perm-p>=0.05 or a bootstrap lift CI including 1.0 on the 'escaping' EU countries, or by the calendar control matching pi (=> seasonality not physics). DATA-RESOLUTION-LIMITED would be refuted by the donor's pi SURVIVING annual coarsening (Part B annual pi still beats calendar+memory) — which would re-open a true EU geographic test. A BOUNDARY verdict would be refuted by acquiring a PER-SAMPLE EU FIB source (e.g. France baignades daily counts) and showing daily-resolution EU pi transfer.

reports/experiment_program/B2/b2_results.title: Upstream-lead sweep: systematic advective escape search across USGS-IV pairs
reports/experiment_program/B2/b2_results.headline: GENERALIZED ESCAPE(S) FOUND: 1 of 24 confirmed pairs clear dAP>+0.03 CI-separated AND survive the block-permutation null -> new advective escapes of the driver-null law. turbidity_fnu 11455350->11455335 dAP=0.1075 CI[0.0593, 0.1399] @1d (perm-p=0.0). Most pairs do NOT clear (driver-null holds broadly).

reports/experiment_program/B5/b5_results.question: Do exogenous drivers lift tail recall / AP on extreme high-consequence exceedances even when they don't on the mean?
reports/experiment_program/B5/b5_results.model_contrast: driver-free (no rain/discharge/air | no nutrients/chl/PN) vs driver-augmented (full A1), same repo HGBT config, isotonic on cal
reports/experiment_program/B5/b5_results.tail_definition: exogenous train-derived p90 severity regimes (wet/dry-onset/station-memory/bloom); recall at fixed precision (0.5, 0.7) + AP on tail
reports/experiment_program/B5/b5_results.gate: recall OR AP gain of driver-aug over driver-free is bootstrap-CI-separated on >=1 driver-relevant tail AND aug beats best-naive there

verdict.interpretation: Drivers earn tail-only skill on the listed strata (CI-separated).
verdict.headline: ESCAPE CONFIRMED ON THE WET TAIL: exogenous drivers (rain/discharge) add CI-separated skill on the storm/wet-season regime (AP +0.060 rain-tail, +0.031 calendar-wet-season; recall@P>=0.7 +0.141), permutation-null clean, beats best-naive. They are NULL/net-negative on the pooled mean and on the dry-onset and station-memory tails. The driver-null law holds on the mean but BREAKS on the high-consequence wet tail.

red_team_robustness.concern_pooled_win_is_cherry_pick: pooled flagged a recall@P>=0.5 +0.0323 CI-sep gain BUT pooled AP gain is -0.0078 (CI -0.013,-0.003) and recall@P>=0.7 is -0.008 (CI-sep negative). Pooled drivers are NET-NEGATIVE -> driver-null law holds on the mean. Pooled is excluded from the wins list; verdict unaffected. Reported straight.
red_team_robustness.concern_selection_on_driver: wet_tail is defined by rain_3d>=p90, which could handicap the rain-blind free model on within-stratum rain gradient. RESOLVED: re-ran on a CALENDAR wet season (Nov-Apr, independent of rain magnitude): aug beats free AP +0.0308 (CI 0.0228,0.0390, frac>0=1.0). The wet-regime driver lift is NOT a selection-on-driver artifact.

driver_block_permutation_null.desc: shuffle the 11 driver cols in TRAIN to break their label link, refit aug, re-score on the real wet_tail; 20 refits.
driver_block_permutation_null.conclusion: real gain exceeds all 20 permutations -> lift is genuine rain/discharge signal, not model capacity from extra columns.

reports/semantic_lakehouse/semantic_shad.text:
# Semantic Shadow Discovery Gauntlet

Run: `ultimate_overnight_20260621`
Domains: `bacteria, hab, whale`
Targets scanned: `25`; usable targets: `23`
Model rows: `126`; correlation rows: `4632`

## Objective Read

Found candidate correlations, but treat them as discovery leads until independently reproduced.

reports/model_lab/champion_claimcard_B.j.purpose: settle the held champion re-freeze on the operational metric (decision-curve net benefit + clustered-bootstrap margin), not pooled AP

claim_card.claim: CatBoost champion upgrade over frozen HGBT (deployable stratum, full drivers)
reasons: passes margin + clustered-bootstrap CI + null/FDR/LOCO + decision-curve

reports/model_lab/champion_claimcard_B.j.decision: RECORD-DON'T-SHIP (operationally) — the AP margin is real and county-robust (clustered CI [0.0041, 0.0193] excludes 0), but at the advisory threshold the net-benefit gain is NEGLIGIBLE (max |delta| 0.00138 < 0.002). The ranking edge is concentrated in the dry/summer slices but does not translate into deployment value, so a frozen-champion swap + new dependency is not justified on operational grounds. The honest case to ship rests on calibration (ECE) + the dry/summer-slice gain, not net benefit.

_meta.question: How many days ahead can we predict FIB beach exceedance with genuine skill OVER STATION MEMORY (a multi-feature memory-only HGBT), honestly? (Not over single-bit persistence — that is the weak baseline a critic caught the prior version overclaiming on.)
_meta.headline_baseline: STATION-MEMORY MODEL: HGBT with the same learner/CV/folds as the main model, trained on ONLY the memory/lab-history features (MEMORY_FEATS: exc_prev/exc_prev2/exc_roll5_prev/exc_roll10_prev/station_prior_rate/station_prior_n/days_since_prev/ent|fec|tot|ecoli_prev+_roll3_prev) — NO rain, NO environment, NO season-beyond-memory, NO geography/context.
_meta.win_criterion: HEADLINE: lead L>=1 where the full model beats the STATION-MEMORY MODEL by a CI-separated paired-AP margin (bootstrap 95% CI excludes 0) — i.e. the environmental/forecast features add real skill over the autocorrelation backbone. The single-bit persistence-at-L + climatology comparison is RETAINED but explicitly labelled the WEAK baseline; a beat over it does NOT define the horizon.
_meta.overclaim_fix: A critic caught the prior version claiming a 3-day horizon by comparing only against single-bit persistence (AP ~0.18-0.20). The honest horizon OVER the station-memory MODEL is ~1 day: real CI-separated win at L=0 (+0.16 AP) and L=1 (+0.08 AP), a TIE at L=2 (~+0.01, CI may touch 0), and a LOSS at L=3 (~-0.03). Beyond ~1 day the full model is just station memory + noise.
_meta.leakage_discipline: lab/memory features use reveal_lag_days=L (predecessor sample date <= t-L); rainfall windows END at t-L (lagged rain, no numerical weather forecast available locally -> antecedent-rain proxy is a CONSERVATIVE lower bound, never leaks; a real precip forecast could only do better). climatology fit on train only. NO same-day or future leakage.
_meta.label: enterococcus
caveats: No local numerical weather forecast: forecast uses LAGGED/antecedent rain (windows end at t-L). This is the honest no-forecast lower bound; a skillful precip forecast at 1-3d could recover some of the lost rain signal.

Under ~weekly beach sampling the lab predecessor is typically already >=7d stale, so persistence-at-L barely changes with L -> the BAR (persistence) is nearly as strong at L=3 as at L=0. Beating it is what would prove forecast skill.
EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO is the honest headline; ALL is reported but inflated by the San Diego/Tijuana 2022 regime break.
Margins use the RAW-ranking model AP (isotonic is monotone -> same ranking). Calibrated AP is reported alongside for deploy-readiness.

forecast_horizon.verdict: HONEST: forecast skill OVER STATION MEMORY = 2 day(s). Environmental/forecast features add CI-separated skill at L=0 and out to lead 2d over the multi-feature station-memory MODEL; beyond that the full model is just station memory + noise (TIE/LOSS). Per-lead margin over the station-memory MODEL: L=0 +0.159 [0.1487,0.1683] WIN; L=1 +0.076 [0.0676,0.084] WIN; L=2 +0.016 [0.0081,0.0233] WIN; L=3 -0.018 [-0.025,-0.0103] LOSS. (The model still trivially beats single-bit persistence-at-L + climatology out to lead 3d, but that is the WEAK baseline — NOT the headline; do NOT claim a 3-day horizon from it.)

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab
project_docs.text:
# Monterey Bay AI Lab

**A multi-hazard coastal warning lab for rare water-quality events — anchored on two decades of Monterey Bay mooring physics.**

The Monterey Bay AI Lab is a **multi-hazard coastal warning lab**. Its two current claimable pillars are **statewide bacterial-exceedance warning** and **domoic-acid / HAB next-visit warning**; both are event-forecasting problems where the model beats an honest operational baseline. The 21-year MBARI M1/M2 mooring record is the physical **backbone** that grounds and calibrates the work, not the forecasting headline.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab
project_docs.text: ## Core Capabilities

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab
project_docs.text:
### 1. Statewide Event-Driven Forecasting — the headline
A multimodal engine predicting rare, non-persistent events (bacterial exceedances) across 800+ California stations.
- **Verified margin:** on a statewide California enterococcus benchmark (1.32M lab **assays** → ~442k modeled station-days, of which ~89k are in the deployable-stratum test split; test events ≥2022), a calibrated gradient-boosted model reaches **ROC-AUC 0.868 / AP 0.5099** on the **statewide deployable stratum** (California marine enterococcus in the standard rainfall/runoff regime) — the honest headline, since the cross-border San Diego/Tijuana plume is a 2022 data-regime artifact and is reported separately, not pooled. It beats the deployed AB411 rain rule (AP 0.18) and a Virtual-Beach-class MLR (AP 0.38), is well-calibrated after isotonic calibration (ECE 0.009 at the operational 2-day lab-result reveal lag — the AP/ECE include the confirmed harmonic spring-neap tidal-range escape, +0.011 AP over rain+discharge; see `signals/catalog.yaml` `tide_range_springneap` — the tracked, byte-reproducible `expected/` outputs; see `MODEL_SUITE.md`), and generalizes spatially: it beats both the regulatory rule and station-memory in **9/9 leave-one-county-out folds**. Decision-support-grade. (The older "AUC 0.888" headline was the artifact-inflated pooled/multi-analyte figure and has been retired — see `research/bacteria/reproduce/PAPER_DRAFT.md`, which derives the honest deployable stratum and the reveal-lag-enforced metrics above.)
- **The honest frontier:** site memory is a strong baseline (a recently-dirty beach tends to stay dirty; station-memory alone reaches AUC ~0.77 / AP 0.28 on the same statewide deployable stratum). The model already beats it meaningfully (+0.23 AP) with environmental drivers, and — verified — **generalizes to beaches it was never trained on**: under leave-one-beach-out cross-validation the driver model holds **AP 0.494 / ROC-AUC 0.852** on never-seen beaches, still beating station-memory (AP 0.28) and the AB411 rule (AP 0.18), so the skill is a transferable environmental pattern, not per-station memorization. The open frontier is extending risk estimates to the beaches we **don't** monitor. A learned spatial surface from *raw* station coordinates or a nearest-neighbor exceedance lag turns out to be a **wash** — no measurable lift (all spatial arms AP 0.497) because the per-station / county / statewide rate features already encode location — while residual per-beach autocorrelation remains (Moran's I ≈ 0.20, one-sided p<0.01). The obvious next lever — **physical spatial covariates** (outfall / river-mouth distance, embayment) — was then tested and is *also* a wash (it underperforms the lat/lon arm by −0.028 AP under leave-one-county-out and does not generalize), so there is currently **no demonstrated lever** that turns the residual clustering into transferable skill; it remains an open problem. These results reproduce from `research/bacteria/spatial_autocorr.py` (leave-one-beach-out + Moran's I), `research/bacteria/spatial_drivers_experiment.py` (the raw-coordinate wash), and `research/bacteria/physical_spatial_covariates.py` (the physical-covariate wash).

**The Apache-2.0 LICENSE covers the code only.** Third-party data retrieved or redistributed
by this project remains under its providers' terms. Verify each source's terms before
redistribution or commercial use.

project_docs.title: Data Provenance & License
project_docs.text:
## Sources

| Dataset | Provider | Access | Terms |
|---|---|---|---|
| M1 / M2 mooring time series | MBARI | OPeNDAP `https://dods.mbari.org/opendap/...` | MBARI data use terms |
| Sea-surface temperature (MUR SST) | NASA JPL PO.DAAC | NOAA MUR cache | NASA open data |
| Buoy waves/wind (NDBC 46042) | NOAA NDBC | `ndbc.noaa.gov` | U.S. Gov public domain |
| Tides / water level (CO-OPS) | NOAA CO-OPS | `tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov` | U.S. Gov public domain |
| Upwelling indices | NOAA | public feeds | U.S. Gov public domain |
| CA beach water-quality (BeachWatch) | CA SWRCB / CDPH | `waterqualitydata.us` / state records | CA public records |
| CalHABMAP pier domoic acid (particulate DA) | CalHABMAP / SCCOOS | ERDDAP `erddap.sccoos.org` | public research data (shipped: `data/external_curated/habmap_cdph/habmap_cdph.parquet`, 304 KB) |

project_docs.title: Data Provenance & License
project_docs.text:
## How the pipeline reads data

Code never hardcodes a path or cloud project. Configure via environment variables:

- `MBAL_PROJECT_ROOT` — repo / working root
- `MBAL_SOURCE_PARQUET` — curated source panel
- `MBAL_CACHE_DIR` — feature/driver cache (`nn_cache`)
- `MBAL_LAKEHOUSE_DIR` — lakehouse outputs (`lakehouse`)
- `MBAL_GCP_PROJECT` — your own GCP project, if pulling from BigQuery

project_docs.title: Model Suite - what exists, what has deps, what is claimable
project_docs.text:
# Model Suite - what exists, what has deps, what is claimable

Generated from `research/model_lab/model_registry.yaml` by `research/model_lab/model_suite.py`.
Fail-closed: a model is **claimable** only if the registry marks it so AND its evidence file exists.

- registry validation: PASS
- models registered: 18
- claimable now: 2

| model | family | task | neural? | deps available? | beats baseline? | calibrated? | claimable? | status | blocker |
|---|---|---|:-:|:-:|---|:-:|:-:|---|---|
| `bacteria_hgbt_isotonic` | tree | bacteria_classification | no | yes | yes - beats AB411, Virtual-Beach MLR, and station-memory | yes | **YES** | production_candidate | prospective forward-time pilot + partner available_at lab timestamps |
| `bacteria_hgbt_spatial` | tree | bacteria_classification | no | yes | no - raw coordinates and neighbour-lag are a wash (all spatial arms AP 0.497 = the no-spatial driver model); per-station/county/statewide rate features already encode location | yes | no | negative_result | raw-coordinate spatial surface does not beat the no-spatial driver model; physical covariates (outfall/river-mouth distance) were also tested and are a wash (-0.0018 AP vs lat/lon on holdout, -0.0283 AP under leave-one-county-out - do not generalize); no demonstrated lever for the residual per-beach clustering |
| `bacteria_xgboost` | tree | bacteria_classification | no | yes | comparator - run via the suite to test the data ceiling vs HGBT | yes | no | benchmark | promote only if it beats the best operational baseline AND calibrates (it won't materially beat HGBT - data ceiling) |
| `bacteria_catboost` | tree | bacteria_classification | no | yes | YES vs HGBT, paired-verified - AP 0.507 vs HGBT 0.4938 (dAP +0.0132), paired bootstrap CI [0.010, 0.0164] EXCLUDES 0, wins 13/15 leave-one-county-out, and better calibrated (ECE 0.0095 vs 0.017). A real, gate-clearing, county-robust model-level boost; champion-UPGRADE candidate pending the freeze/reproduction gate. | yes | no | benchmark | claimable only after the champion-reproducibility + freeze gate re-crowns it (approval-boundary call); the boost is modest (+2.7% rel, still ~0.50 / data-ceilinged) and from one fixed-seed split - confirm under seed/snapshot variation before promotion |
| `bacteria_lightgbm` | tree | bacteria_classification | no | yes | YES vs HGBT, paired-verified - AP 0.5019 vs 0.4938 (dAP +0.0081), paired bootstrap CI [0.0057, 0.0105] EXCLUDES 0, wins 14/15 leave-one-county-out. Real gate-clearing boost, smaller than CatBoost; second confirmation that the tree ceiling is ~0.50-0.51. | yes | no | benchmark | modest (+0.008 AP) and one fixed-seed split; CatBoost is the stronger upgrade candidate. Promotion still needs the freeze/reproduction gate. |
| `bacteria_stack` | tree | bacteria_classification | no | yes | comparator - the blend most likely to beat a single plateaued model; measured in the all-data suite rerun | yes | no | benchmark | promote only on a material, repeated lift over HGBT with deployable calibration |
| `bacteria_mlp_plr` | neural | bacteria_classification | yes | yes | comparator - fair modern deep-tabular test; measured in the all-data suite rerun | yes | no | benchmark | promote only if it materially beats HGBT AND calibrates (prior: an earlier plain entity-emb MLP tied HGBT) |
| `bacteria_tabpfn_matched` | foundation | bacteria_classification | yes | yes | comparator only - answers 'did you try a tabular foundation model?' (NEW_LANGUAGES_TEST_PLAN item 63). Expected within noise of HGBT per the data ceiling; not a claim. | yes | no | benchmark | NOT a plain model_suite factory by design: the suite trains on the full ~270k-row pre-2020 set, but TabPFN caps at ~50k (v2 ~10k), so a bare drop-in would confound model class with train-set size. The matched-n harness fixes this (subsample both arms identically). Promote only if tabpfn_matched materially beats hgbt_matched at matched n AND calibration holds. |
| `xgboost_forecast_v2` | tree | m1_forecasting | no | yes | horizon-specific - gated by best-naive (ops/seasonal_naive.py); +72h tier rejected | no | no | benchmark | promotion is per target/horizon cell via release_gate/mbal_promotion_matrix.py |
| `patchtst` | neural | m1_forecasting | yes | yes | horizon-specific; best aggregate full-history neural run | no | no | benchmark | must clear the best-naive gate per cell |
| `nhits` | neural | m1_forecasting | yes | yes | not established | no | no | research_candidate | no validated win over best-naive |
| `early_warning_cusum` | detector | bacteria_event_detection | no | yes | NO - a NULL result; the SD-2022 break is a ~35-sigma step any detector flags | no | no | negative_result | n/a - documented null; do not revive as a contribution |
| `ab411_rain_rule` | baseline | bacteria_classification | no | yes | n/a - this is the deployed practice we must beat (AP ~0.18 on the deployable stratum) | no | no | baseline | n/a |

The earlier 'beats C-HARM 8/8, ~10x' framing is a BROKEN-BASELINE MIRAGE (C-HARM is sub-random here, ROC-AUC 0.30) - retained only as the operational-incumbent comparison. project_docs.title: Model Suite - what exists, what has deps, what is claimable
project_docs.text:
## Legend
- **neural?** - `neural`/`foundation`/`continual` families are neural; `tree`/`baseline` are not. (The `continual` family is reserved and currently has no registered model: the dense mixture-of-experts design once kept here was retired as bloat, and what survives is a compact 2,772,504-param Conv1d+MLP forecaster in `sota_continual_learning/`, not registered as a claimable model.)
- **deps available?** - whether the model's declared dependencies import in this environment. This is not an execution smoke test for the registry entrypoint.
- **beats baseline?** - against the honest baseline (best-naive for forecasting; AB411 / Virtual-Beach MLR / station-memory for bacteria), stated straight.
- **claimable?** - safe to put in a pitch/paper. Only `production_candidate` (and, case-by-case, a promoted `benchmark`) with on-disk evidence qualifies.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Readiness Register
project_docs.text:
# Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Readiness Register

Last verified: 2026-06-13

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Readiness Register
project_docs.text:
## Decision

Status: **local production gate passed; senior-ready gate is held on true external-smoke evidence and current high-risk worktree state.**

The project is now shaped as a production candidate: source control is initialized,
generated artifacts are ignored, unit tests pass, release-gate tooling exists, and the
portable CPU smoke job succeeded from `C:\mbal_portable_smoke`. It is not approved for
external deployment, global SOTA claims, or broad driver-value claims. Promotion remains
target/horizon-specific, and the senior gate must remain below `PASS` until an external
runner writes `release_gate/reports/external_smoke_report.json` with runner-owned storage
paths and the in-flight worktree is reduced below the senior-gate risk threshold.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Readiness Register
project_docs.text:
## Verified Local State

- Editable install: `python -m pip install -e .[test]` succeeded.
- Test suite: `python ops\run_tests.py` passes in the current local verification.
- Release gate: `python release_gate\mbal_release_gate.py --project-root . --output-dir release_gate\reports` returns `PASS`.
- Daily learnings: `python ops\generate_daily_learnings.py --project-root .` writes `archive\reports\MBAL_AI_DAILY_LEARNINGS.md`.
- Senior gate: `python release_gate\mbal_sr_manager_gate.py --project-root . --output release_gate\reports\sr_manager_gate_report.json --no-exit-code` returns `WARN` until `external_smoke_report.json` exists and passes, and until the git worktree has fewer than 20 changed/untracked paths.
- GPU stack: RTX 5090 CUDA/Torch/XGBoost checks pass in the release gate.
- Curated dataset: validation report matches 3,807,370 expected rows across 351 Parquet files.
- Promotion matrix: 5,566 rows with 39 `promote`, 12 `candidate_split_mismatch`, 877 `reject`, and 4,638 `insufficient_data`; the 39 promoted rows collapse to 12 unique target/horizon cells and 34 unique model cells.
- Portable smoke: `python .\mbal_train.py --jobs .\ops\jobs.smoke.json --accel cpu --no-wake-lock --no-gpu-preflight --force` passed from `C:\mbal_portable_smoke\src` with `scored_rows=4`, `summary.json`, `leaderboard.csv`, `cv_predictions.parquet`, and lakehouse artifacts.
- Artifact audit: smoke outputs contain no absolute project/data paths from the local working tree. The raw process log contains the local Python installation path from a third-party warning; this is runtime-environment noise, not a project artifact dependency.
- **Hardware Safety**: Training is protected by `ops/mbal_sota_utils.py:SafetyMonitor`, enforcing an 80% VRAM ceiling, 72h runtime limit, and 30m idle shutdown.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts
project_docs.text:
# Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts

Generated: 2026-06-07

This project uses a production-grade local lakehouse architecture. Neural runs emit
structured artifacts under `lakehouse/` ensuring the workflow remains portable to
generic remote compute environments (Spark, Kubernetes, object storage, or managed
runtime platforms) without changing the core modeling contract.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts
project_docs.text:
## Layers

- `lakehouse/silver/forecast_splits/`
  Canonical split contracts. Each `split_id` records the expanding-window cutoffs,
  train window bounds, evaluated horizons, source fingerprint, and run configuration.

- `lakehouse/gold/forecast_runs/`
  Run manifests. Each `run_id` records model, loss, driver configuration, source
  fingerprint, cache version, environment knobs, and artifact paths.

- `lakehouse/gold/forecast_metrics/`
  Normalized target/horizon metrics keyed by `run_id`, `split_id`, `unique_id`,
  and `horizon_h`. Carries the honest-baseline columns `seasonal_naive_rmse` and
  `skill_vs_best_naive_pct` (skill vs the better of persistence and seasonal-naive),
  backfilled by `ops/backfill_seasonal_naive.py` from the prediction partitions.

- `lakehouse/gold/forecast_predictions/`
  Per-run prediction partitions keyed by `run_id`. These preserve the original
  `cv_predictions.parquet` shape while adding `run_id`, `split_id`, and prediction
  column metadata.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts
project_docs.text:
## Architectural Tier Mapping

- **Bronze (Raw/Ingest):**
  Immutable copies of raw MBARI source data and external driver inputs.

- **Silver (Curated/Enriched):**
  QC-applied hourly matrix, observed mask, driver availability table, and
  `forecast_splits`. These are ready for modeling and feature engineering.

- **Gold (Aggregated/Insights):**
  `forecast_runs`, `forecast_metrics`, `forecast_predictions`, and final comparison
  leaderboards. These are consumer-ready artifacts for scientists and managers.

project_docs.title: Cyano Onset Claim-Card Red-Team
project_docs.text:
# Cyano Onset Claim-Card Red-Team

**Verdict:** CLAIMABLE_BUT_SCOPED

Latitude/quadratic geography is a real cold-start cyano-onset law under held-out state, lat-band, and year tests; do not sell the weak photoperiod-only mechanism, and treat winter met as a secondary hardening path.

project_docs.title: Cyano Onset Claim-Card Red-Team
project_docs.text:
## Hard Checks
- Original leave-one-state lat-quad skill: 0.2018 CI [0.0442, 0.3151]
- Excluding 2026 leave-one-state lat-quad skill: 0.2005 CI [0.0426, 0.2978]
- Prior-winter met join rows: 11,240
- Met materially adds to lat-quad: True

project_docs.title: Cyano Onset Claim-Card Red-Team
project_docs.text:
## Claim Discipline
- Claim onset timing, not abundance.
- Anchor on lat/quadratic held-out skill, not photoperiod-only.
- Use as a sampling-start prior for cold-start lakes.

project_docs.title: Domoic-acid forecast -- next-visit pDA >= 0.5 ng/mL (=500 ng/L), ~1-week lead
project_docs.text:
# Domoic-acid forecast -- next-visit pDA >= 0.5 ng/mL (=500 ng/L), ~1-week lead

Train <= 2018 (n=3209, 148 events) | calib 2019-2020 | test > 2020 (n=1837, 52 events, base 0.028).

project_docs.title: Domoic-acid forecast -- next-visit pDA >= 0.5 ng/mL (=500 ng/L), ~1-week lead
project_docs.text:
## PRIMARY UNIT -- toxic ONSET on prior-clean cells (exc_prev==0)

The decision-relevant unit: forecasting a toxic event when the prior visit was CLEAN. Persistence is DEGENERATE here (exc_prev==0 => p=0 on every row), so the fair baseline is max(seasonal-naive, station-memory) -- NEVER persistence.

- model AP **0.1347** vs strongest non-degenerate baseline `seasonal_naive` AP 0.0259 => **margin +0.1089**
- station-clustered bootstrap CI95 [0.0566, 0.2078] (excludes 0, 100% boot+); perm-null p=0.002; N=1785, 34 onset events
- **Verdict: CI-SEPARATED POSITIVE**

project_docs.title: Domoic-acid forecast -- next-visit pDA >= 0.5 ng/mL (=500 ng/L), ~1-week lead
project_docs.text:
## Pooled time-held-out test (SECONDARY context -- AP, base rate ~3%)

Pooled AP mixes the trivial prior-exceed carry-over with onset; the strongest pooled baseline is persistence, so the pooled margin understates the onset skill above.

Feature-group ablation: the physical/nutrient **drivers HURT** (AP drops vs the lean set) -- so the headline model is `DA+precursor`, not the full driver matrix. Same driver-null pattern as M1 / news / wave-tide.

| method | AP | ROC-AUC | AP lift vs base |
|---|--:|--:|--:|
| seasonal_naive | 0.0369 | 0.6072 | 0.8 |
| persistence | 0.1383 | 0.6636 | 3.0 |
| station_memory | 0.0331 | 0.5499 | 0.72 |
| model_DA_history | 0.2133 | 0.8642 | 4.62 |
| model_DA+precursor (headline) | 0.2034 | 0.8421 | 4.41 |
| model_DA+precursor+drivers | 0.1634 | 0.8302 | 3.54 |

**Pooled verdict:** BEATS baselines -- pooled model AP 0.2034 vs best pooled baseline 0.1383 (persistence; see PRIMARY onset unit above for the headline).

**Leave-one-station-out:** model beats seasonal-naive in 8/8 stations.

| station | n | events | model AP | seasonal AP | beats |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|---|
| MontereyWharf | 275 | 49 | 0.5664 | 0.2437 | Y |
| TrinidadPier | 107 | 10 | 0.4222 | 0.0969 | Y |
| StearnsWharf | 883 | 29 | 0.2106 | 0.075 | Y |
| SantaMonicaPier | 896 | 13 | 0.1992 | 0.0324 | Y |
| SantaCruzWharf | 713 | 53 | 0.1778 | 0.1278 | Y |
| NewportBeachPier | 866 | 12 | 0.1305 | 0.0362 | Y |
| ScrippsPier | 919 | 10 | 0.1204 | 0.0257 | Y |
| CalPolyPier | 847 | 25 | 0.107 | 0.0366 | Y |

project_docs.title: MBAI Remote Research & Data Access Contract
project_docs.text:
# MBAI Remote Research & Data Access Contract

This document defines how the Monterey Bay AI Lab (MBAI) handles scaling normalized signals while protecting local compute resources and managing Google Cloud costs.

project_docs.title: MBAI Remote Research & Data Access Contract
project_docs.text:
## 1. The "Forge" vs. The "Evidence Layer"

We operate on a **Push-Only Architecture**.

project_docs.title: MBAI Remote Research & Data Access Contract
project_docs.text:
### The MBAI Forge (User's Machine / RTX 5090)
- **Role:** High-Performance Training (HPT), data normalization, and heuristic fusion.
- **Security:** Private and isolated. **No inbound traffic allowed.**
- **Workflow:** 
  1. Ingest raw data from Google BigQuery (one-time or scheduled sync).
  2. Perform multivariate regression and A/B testing locally.
  3. Emit normalized **Gold Artifacts** (Parquet files).
  4. **Push** Gold Artifacts to the Google Cloud Evidence Layer.

project_docs.title: Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?
project_docs.text:
# Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?

Binding for all agents (Codex, Claude, Gemini, Qwen). Run this gate **before you
suggest OR execute** any new feature, module, abstraction, driver, data source, model,
metric, doc, or "phase." Its job: keep the project continually improving **without**
accreting bloat. Cite the result with a **falsifiable payload** — a bare "beats baseline"
is itself a gate failure (it's how cargo-cult citation gets caught):
*"Value gate: DO_NOW — beats <baseline> by <Δmetric>, test <path::name>"* or
*"rejected: <kill-category> — <one-line reason>"*.

This gate is itself held to its own bar: it is one short checklist, not a manifesto.

project_docs.title: Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?
project_docs.text:
## The 30-second gate — all five must be YES to build

1. **Beats a real baseline / answers a real question.** Does it measurably improve a
   result against an *honest* baseline (seasonal-naive, persistence, AB411, prior-lab,
   station-memory, the current metric) — or answer a question a stakeholder actually
   asked? "It's more sophisticated / more SOTA / more general" is **not** a yes.
2. **Additive & reversible — OR a correctness fix.** New *behavior* lands behind a
   default-off flag or as a new file and is removable cleanly (Delta phases, shadow
   dual-write off-by-default: yes; an in-place rewrite of the gold layer for a new
   feature: no). **Correctness fixes** (leakage, dedup key, median-not-mean, causality)
   are EXEMPT — they may be in-place and one-way, and fixing them is mandatory
   regardless of reversibility (see the data-correctness invariants in `AGENTS.md`).
3. **Testable, and you will test it.** Is there a concrete pass/fail test or a
   reproducible number you will add in the same change? If you can't test it, you can't
   claim it.
4. **Valuable NOW, not speculative.** Its value must be demonstrable now, not unprovable
   until some future step lands. Sequencing is fine — building a holdout harness *before*
   the validation it enables passes; building a feature whose worth can't be shown until
   a burn-in/dataset/evidence step that hasn't happened does not. When in doubt, do the
   cheaper enabling step first.
5. **Genuinely new, and improves a result we actually claim.** Not redundant with an
   existing path, and not *polishing* a case we deliberately **exclude** (e.g. tuning San
   Diego's calibration) or a stratum we don't deploy. **Carve-out:** work that would
   *change* the exclusion decision — expand coverage, or fix the reason a region was
   excluded — is a baseline-beating feature and PASSES.

If any answer is NO → **do not build it.** State which kill-category it is.

project_docs.title: Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?
project_docs.text:
## Kill list — name the reason when you reject something

- **Bloat** — needless complexity/abstraction; recovers what a simpler thing already gives.
- **Vapor** — scaffolding not wired to real work; a "framework" with no caller.
- **Premature** — depends on a burn-in / evidence / data step not yet done.
- **Ops-toggle** — a config/env flip or one-line invocation, not an engineering deliverable.
- **Redundant** — an existing path already covers it.
- **Cosmetic** — renames, reformatting, micro-metrics that change no decision.
- **Excluded-case polish** — optimizes a region/stratum/path we deliberately set aside
  *without changing the exclusion decision* (tuning excluded San Diego's calibration).
  NB: work that makes an excluded case *includable* is NOT this — it passes (see Q5).

project_docs.title: Open-Source Readiness
project_docs.text:
# Open-Source Readiness

This checklist defines the minimum bar before publishing the repository publicly.

project_docs.title: Open-Source Readiness
project_docs.text:
## Required

- `README.md` explains the project, setup, tests, Docker workflow, and data boundaries.
- `LICENSE` grants explicit open-source rights.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` explains branch, test, and pull request expectations.
- `SECURITY.md` gives a private vulnerability reporting path.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` sets collaboration expectations.
- `pyproject.toml` declares package metadata and dependency groups.
- `Dockerfile` and `.dockerignore` support clean reproducibility checks.
- Tests pass with `python ops/run_tests.py`.

project_docs.title: Open-Source Readiness
project_docs.text:
## Data Boundary

Do not publish private credentials, local `.env` files, raw protected datasets, generated caches, or workstation-only artifacts. Public releases should include one of the following:

- Small synthetic fixtures.
- Publicly licensed sample data.
- Scripts that download public data from documented sources.
- Precomputed public artifacts with provenance and version notes.

project_docs.title: MBAL Public-Repo Publish Runbook
project_docs.text:
# MBAL Public-Repo Publish Runbook

**Status:** prep-complete; the destructive history purge is **NOT** run by this runbook
automatically — it is gated behind explicit owner approval (see Step 4).

This runbook is the **executable, fail-closed** sequence for cutting the public open-source
release of the Monterey Bay AI Lab repo. It exists because the working repo carries things a
public repo must never have:

- ~3.9 GB of raw third-party data (the 50 `data/external_raw/dpr_pur/pur19xx.zip` PUR zips,
  16 of them >100 MB — these also block `git push` to GitHub entirely),
- committed `*.xlsx` workbooks and other large/binary blobs in **history**,
- a personal contact email and private GCP identifiers (project `monterey-bay-lab`, the two
  GCS buckets, the BigQuery dataset) hardcoded in source,
- third-party collaborator PII under `docs/collab/` (untracked today, but must be kept out).

The non-negotiable rule: **nothing leaves this tree until `ops/prepublish_gate.py` prints
`RESULT: PASS`.** The gate is fail-closed (any blocker → exit 1).

---

project_docs.title: MBAL Public-Repo Publish Runbook
project_docs.text:
## Canonical ignore-set

`release/publish/gitignore.public` is the **canonical** public ignore-set and a **strict
superset** of the repo-root `.gitignore`. Step 2 copies it over the public tree as
`.gitignore`. `ops/prepublish_gate.py` Check 4 enforces the superset invariant — if you add a
rule to root `.gitignore`, mirror it into `gitignore.public` or the gate fails.

---

project_docs.title: MBAL Public-Repo Publish Runbook
project_docs.text:
## Step 0 — Scrub source (REL-04) and re-run until green

These are non-destructive content fixes on the working tree.

1. **Personal email → env var.** Done for the 5 fetcher/adapter files
   (`ops/find_open_datasets.py`, `ops/fetch_research_corpus.py`,
   `research/whale/global_mortality.py`, `research/whale/fetch_global_ocean.py`,
   `ops/data_fetch/adapters/ais_marinecadastre.py`): each now reads
   `os.environ.get("MBAL_CONTACT_EMAIL", "contact@example.org")`. Set `MBAL_CONTACT_EMAIL`
   in the environment for real runs.
2. **Remaining email / GCP literals.** The gate will list every *other* tracked file that
   still contains a personal email or a private GCP literal (`monterey-bay-lab`,
   `monterey-bay-lab-lakehouse`, `monterey-bay-lab-archive`, `blue_current_core_v2`). Those
   files are owned by OTHER remediation lanes — they must be scrubbed by their owners (e.g.
   `ops/cloud_sync.py`, `ops/run_cloud_sync.ps1`, `ops/run_daily_nowcast.ps1`,
   `research/bacteria/*`, `baylens/src/llm_client.py`, `TRAINING_DATA_VIZ.html`,
   `docs/collab/LAKEHOUSE_READONLY_ACCESS.md`). Do not publish until they are clean.

```bash
python ops/prepublish_gate.py --json release/publish/prepublish_report.json
```

Re-run after each scrub. **Do not proceed while any BLOCKER remains** other than the
big-blob/denied-path blockers that Step 4's purge is designed to remove.

project_docs.text:
# BEATING DEPLOYED PRACTICE IN BEACH-WATER QUALITY NOWCASTING: A LEAKAGE-CLEAN, CALIBRATED, STATEWIDE BENCHMARK ON THE CALIFORNIA MARINE ENTEROCOCCUS STANDARD

**Author:** Jon Dinneen
**Affiliation:** [to be completed]
**Correspondence:** [email]
**Draft for submission — not peer reviewed. Prepared 2026-06-09.**

> Reproduction materials for every number in this draft are in the same directory:
> see `REPRODUCE.md`. The headline configuration is
> `--label enterococcus --reveal-lag-days 2`; expected outputs are in `expected/`.

Suggested venues (any one; this is written venue-neutral as a short paper / extended abstract):
NeurIPS / ICLR "Tackling Climate Change with ML" workshop; ACM COMPASS; Environmental
Modelling & Software; Water Research; AGU Ocean Sciences; KDD Applied Data Science track.

---

project_docs.title: BEATING DEPLOYED PRACTICE IN BEACH-WATER QUALITY NOWCASTING: A LEAKAGE-CLEAN, CALIBRATED, STATEWIDE BENCHMARK ON THE CAL
project_docs.text:
## ABSTRACT

Beach advisories for fecal-indicator-bacteria (FIB) exceedance are issued from laboratory
cultures that return roughly 18-24 hours after a water sample is collected, so the same-day
public-health decision is effectively unguided or relies on a coarse wet-weather rule
(California AB411). We build a statewide California benchmark (1.32M assays, 802 monitoring
stations, 17 counties, 2005-2026) for NOWCASTING single-sample marine enterococcus
exceedance (>104 MPN/100 mL) using only information available at sampling time. With strictly
causal features — prior laboratory results enforced against the true lab-reveal lag, station
memory, seasonality, gridded rainfall, and USGS river first-flush discharge — a gradient-
boosted decision-tree model (with a harmonic spring-neap tidal-range feature), isotonically
calibrated, attains Average Precision (AP) 0.510 /
AUROC 0.868 on the honest statewide stratum. It beats, on the same data: the AB411 rain rule
(AP 0.178; ~2.8x), a Virtual-Beach-class per-site multiple logistic regression representing
DEPLOYED practice (AP 0.375; +0.12 AP, ~32%), and a station-memory baseline (AP 0.278) —
while remaining deploy-ready in calibration (Expected Calibration Error, ECE, 0.020). Under
leave-one-county-out (LOCO) spatial cross-validation, the model beats AB411 and station
memory in 9/9 held-out counties. We report negative and null results honestly: a 2022
San Diego / Tijuana-River sewage REGIME BREAK inflates pooled metrics and is excluded from
the headline; river discharge is largely rain-redundant; and a static probability calibrator
degrades under regime shift, which a prequential (online) recalibrator only partially repairs.
We release a leakage-controlled, reproducible benchmark and argue that the field's
publishable contribution is not a higher pooled AUC but a calibrated model that beats deployed
practice under honest, stratified, spatially-held-out evaluation.

---

project_docs.title: BEATING DEPLOYED PRACTICE IN BEACH-WATER QUALITY NOWCASTING: A LEAKAGE-CLEAN, CALIBRATED, STATEWIDE BENCHMARK ON THE CAL
project_docs.text:
## 1. INTRODUCTION

Recreational coastal waters are monitored for fecal-indicator bacteria; when a single-sample
standard is exceeded, an advisory or closure is posted. The operational bottleneck is time:
the culture-based laboratory assay returns ~18-24 hours after sampling, so on the day a
beachgoer is exposed, the manager either has yesterday's result or, under California's AB411
program, a wet-weather rule that posts an advisory when rainfall exceeds a threshold. AB411 is
known to be weak; the predictive tool actually deployed at many modern beach programs is EPA
Virtual Beach, a per-site multiple-linear/logistic regression on local hydrometeorological
drivers, or a USGS site-specific nowcast.

A large body of work reports machine-learning models for FIB nowcasting, often with
impressive pooled AUC. Two failure modes recur and motivate this paper. First, LEAKAGE: many
"prior result" features are constructed by sample index (the previous SAMPLE), not by reveal
time (the previous RESULT that had physically returned by prediction time); and spatially
pooled features can leak a held-out region into training. Second, ARTIFACTS: a model evaluated
across a measurement/program discontinuity can score the discontinuity rather than generalizable
skill, inflating a headline number that does not survive domain scrutiny.

Our contribution is an honest benchmark. We (i) define a single, standard-aligned target — the
AB411 marine enterococcus single-sample standard; (ii) construct strictly causal features with
an enforced lab-reveal lag; (iii) evaluate against a baseline ladder that includes a
Virtual-Beach-class model representing deployed practice, not only the weak AB411 rule;
(iv) stratify by region and era to surface, rather than hide, a regime break; (v) test spatial
generalization by leave-one-county-out; and (vi) treat calibration as an explicit deploy gate.
We find a model that beats deployed practice and generalizes — and we report where it does not.

---
